dilution.value: Low pH; may cause physical instability with other compounds

administration.label: Route
administration.value: Intravenous infusion

administration.label: Infusion rate
administration.value: No more than 10 mg/min

administration.label: Minimum infusion duration
administration.value: At least 60 minutes per dose, whichever is longer

administration.label: Rate restrictions
administration.value: Concentrations no more than 5 mg/mL and rates no more than 10 mg/min recommended; use of concentrations up to 10 mg/mL may increase risk of infusion-related events

administration.label: Method
administration.value: Intermittent infusion is the recommended method of administration

administration.label: Infusion-related events
administration.value: Related to both concentration and rate; may occur at any rate or concentration

ivCompatibility.label: Beta-lactam antibiotics
ivCompatibility.value: Physically incompatible when mixed; likelihood of precipitation increases with higher vancomycin concentrations

ivCompatibility.label: Ceftazidime (intravitreal)
ivCompatibility.value: Precipitation reported after intravitreal injection using different syringes and needles

ivCompatibility.label: IV line flushing
ivCompatibility.value: Adequately flush intravenous lines between administration of vancomycin and other antibiotics

ivPush.warning: Rapid bolus may cause exaggerated hypotension, shock, and rarely cardiac arrest. Must be infused over at least 60 minutes at no more than 10 mg/min (PI).

monitoring.labs: Renal function (serum creatinine, creatinine clearance); vancomycin serum concentrations especially in seriously ill patients with changing renal function, premature infants, neonates, pediatric patients, elderly, and patients with impaired renal function
monitoring.drugLevels: Vancomycin serum concentrations can be determined by microbiologic assay, radioimmunoassay, fluorescence polarization immunoassay, fluorescence immunoassay, or high-pressure liquid chromatography; therapeutic monitoring recommended for serious MRSA infections per ASHP/IDSA/PIDS/SIDP consensus guideline
monitoring.duringInfusion: Monitor for infusion-related events (related to concentration and rate of administration)

clinicalAlerts: After reconstitution further dilution is required before use
Oxygen sensitive product
Not effective by oral route for systemic infections (oral route for C. difficile colitis only)
Dosage adjustment required in renal impairment and elderly patients
Vancomycin solution has low pH and may cause physical instability when mixed with other compounds
Beta-lactam antibiotics are physically incompatible with vancomycin solutions
Higher concentrations (up to 10 mg/mL) may increase risk of infusion-related events

dosageAndTitration.label: Usual adult IV dose

dosageAndTitration.value: Dose in mg/day = 15 x GFR (mL/min). CrCl 100: 1,545 mg/day; CrCl 50: 770 mg/day; CrCl 10: 155 mg/day

dosageAndTitration.label: Anuria dosing
dosageAndTitration.value: 1,000 mg every 7 to 10 days; maintenance 1.9 mg/kg/24 hr to maintain stable concentrations

dosageAndTitration.label: Infusion rate limit
dosageAndTitration.value: No more than 10 mg/min; each dose over at least 60 minutes, whichever is longer

abaloparatide.drugClass: PTH1 receptor agonist / bone anabolic agent
abaloparatide.sourceNotes: Data extracted from FDA package insert (DailyMed) for abaloparatide. No ASHP guidelines consulted as drug is not formulated for IV administration.

administration.label: Route
administration.value: Subcutaneous only into periumbilical region of abdomen

administration.label: Dosage
administration.value: 80 mcg once daily

administration.label: Do NOT administer
administration.value: Intravenously or intramuscularly

administration.label: Initial doses
administration.value: Administer where patient can sit or lie down if necessary, in case symptoms of orthostatic hypotension occur

administration.label: Timing
administration.value: Rotate injection site daily; administer at approximately the same time every day

administration.label: Inspection
administration.value: Clear and colorless solution. Do not use if solid particles appear or if solution is cloudy or colored

monitoring.vitals: Orthostatic blood pressure monitoring recommended, especially after first injection

sideEffects: Injection site reactions (in men: erythema, swelling, pain)
clinicalAlerts: Do not administer intravenously or intramuscularly - subcutaneous route only
Orthostatic hypotension may occur; administer initial doses where patient can sit or lie down
Treatment duration should not exceed 2 years during a patient's lifetime
Patients should receive supplemental calcium and vitamin D if dietary intake is inadequate
41% of patients developed anti-abaloparatide antibodies during 18-month treatment

clinicalAlerts: CALQUENCE is formulated as tablets for oral administration only. No intravenous formulation exists. Each tablet contains 100 mg acalabrutinib (equivalent to 129 mg acalabrutinib maleate). Tablets must be swallowed whole with water; patients should not chew, crush, dissolve, or cut tablets.

acetaminophen.sourceNotes: Data extracted from FDA package insert accessed via DailyMed; ASHP sources provided did not contain information relevant to IV acetaminophen.

dilution.value: 10 mg/mL in 100 mL (1000 mg) or 65 mL (650 mg) bags

dilution.label: For doses <1000 mg
dilution.value: Withdraw appropriate dose from intact sealed container and place into separate empty sterile container (glass bottle, plastic IV container, or syringe) prior to administration

dilution.value: May be administered without further dilution for 1000 mg doses in patients ≥50 kg

dilution.label: Small volume pediatric doses
dilution.value: Doses up to 60 mL may be placed in syringe for administration via syringe pump

dilution.label: Stability after container opened
dilution.value: Once container seal penetrated or contents transferred, administer within 6 hours

dilution.label: Osmolality
dilution.value: Approximately 290 mOsm/kg

dilution.label: pH
dilution.value: Approximately 5.0 to 6.3

administration.label: Route
administration.value: Intravenous infusion only

administration.label: Infusion duration
administration.value: 15 minutes

administration.label: Rate restriction
administration.value: Must be administered over 15 minutes; do not administer as IV push or bolus

administration.label: Monitoring during infusion
administration.value: Monitor end of infusion to prevent possibility of air embolism, especially when acetaminophen is primary infusion

administration.label: Administration technique
administration.value: For ≥50 kg patients requiring 1000 mg: insert non-vented IV set through administration spike port of 100 mL bag. For small volume pediatric doses up to 60 mL: use syringe pump

administration.label: Admixture restriction
administration.value: Do not add other medications to acetaminophen injection solution or infusion device

ivCompatibility.label: Diazepam

ivCompatibility.label: Chlorpromazine hydrochloride
ivCompatibility.value: Physically incompatible; do not administer simultaneously

monitoring.duringInfusion: Monitor end of infusion to prevent possibility of air embolism, especially when acetaminophen injection is primary infusion

blackBoxWarnings: Risk of medication errors: Ensure dose in mg and mL is not confused; dosing is weight-based for patients under 50 kg; infusion pumps are properly programmed; total daily dose from all sources does not exceed maximum daily limits

Hepatotoxicity: Acetaminophen associated with cases of acute liver failure, at times resulting in liver transplant and death. Most cases associated with doses exceeding maximum daily limits and often involve more than one acetaminophen-containing product
clinicalAlerts: Exceeding maximum mg/kg daily dose may result in hepatic injury, including risk of liver failure and death
Ensure total acetaminophen from all routes (IV, oral, rectal) and all sources does not exceed maximum recommended dose
Contraindicated in patients with severe hepatic impairment or severe active liver disease
Entire 100 mL container not intended for use in patients weighing less than 50 kg
Single-dose container: unused portion must be discarded
Use aseptic technique when preparing for IV infusion

Paresthesia (numbness and tingling of extremities and face)
Increased risk of nephrolithiasis with long-term therapy
clinicalAlerts: Fatalities have occurred due to severe reactions to sulfonamides including Stevens-Johnson syndrome, toxic epidermal necrolysis, fulminant hepatic necrosis, agranulocytosis, aplastic anemia, other blood dyscrasias, and anaphylaxis
Caution advised for patients receiving concomitant high-dose aspirin and acetazolamide, as anorexia, tachypnea, lethargy, metabolic acidosis, coma, and death have been reported
Contraindicated in patients with cirrhosis because of risk of hepatic encephalopathy
Choroidal effusion and choroidal detachment reported after use in post-operative period following ophthalmic surgery; discontinue if suspected
May precipitate or aggravate acidosis in patients with pulmonary obstruction or emphysema where alveolar ventilation may be impaired

acyclovir.drugClass: Synthetic nucleoside analog, antiviral agent active against herpes viruses
acyclovir.sourceNotes: Data extracted from FDA package insert (DailyMed setid 23a7cf9e-f21b-06c2-e063-6394a90aa623); ASHP guidelines did not contain relevant acyclovir IV administration information.

dilution.label: Compatible Diluents
dilution.value: Standard, commercially available electrolyte and glucose solutions are suitable for intravenous administration; biologic or colloidal fluids (e.g., blood products, protein solutions, etc.) are not recommended

dilution.label: Final Concentration
dilution.value: Infusion concentrations of approximately 7 mg/mL or lower are recommended. Higher concentrations (e.g., 10 mg/mL) may produce phlebitis or inflammation at the injection site upon inadvertent extravasation

dilution.label: Volume
dilution.value: In clinical studies, the average 70 kg adult received between 60 and 150 mL of fluid per dose

dilution.label: Stability After Dilution
dilution.value: Once diluted for administration, each dose should be used within 24 hours

administration.label: Route
administration.value: Intravenous infusion only. Must NOT be administered topically, intramuscularly, orally, subcutaneously, or in the eye

administration.label: Infusion Duration
administration.value: Must be infused at a constant rate over at least 1 hour

administration.label: Rate Restrictions
administration.value: RAPID OR BOLUS INTRAVENOUS INJECTION MUST BE AVOIDED to reduce the risk of renal tubular damage

ivCompatibility.label: Biologic or Colloidal Fluids
ivCompatibility.value: Blood products, protein solutions

ivCompatibility.label: Electrolyte and Glucose Solutions
ivCompatibility.value: Standard, commercially available electrolyte and glucose solutions

monitoring.labs: Serum creatinine, BUN, transaminases, CBC (for anemia, neutropenia, thrombocytopenia, thrombocytosis, leukocytosis, neutrophilia)
monitoring.duringInfusion: Monitor infusion site for inflammation or phlebitis; monitor for signs of extravasation

sideEffects: Inflammation or phlebitis at injection site (approximately 9%)
Transient elevations of serum creatinine or BUN (5% to 10%, higher incidence with rapid infusion)
Nausea and/or vomiting (approximately 7%, majority in nonhospitalized patients receiving 10 mg/kg)
Hematologic abnormalities: anemia, neutropenia, thrombocytopenia, thrombocytosis, leukocytosis, neutrophilia (less than 1%)
clinicalAlerts: Renal failure, in some cases resulting in death, has been observed with acyclovir therapy
Thrombotic thrombocytopenic purpura/hemolytic uremic syndrome (TTP/HUS), which has resulted in death, has occurred in immunocompromised patients receiving acyclovir therapy
Intravenous infusions must be given over at least 1 hour to reduce risk of renal tubular damage
Rapid or bolus intravenous injection must be avoided
Intramuscular or subcutaneous injection must be avoided
Maximum dose equivalent to 20 mg/kg every 8 hours should not be exceeded for any patient
Contraindicated in patients who develop hypersensitivity to acyclovir or valacyclovir

citation.primary: Acyclovir Sodium Injection [package insert]. DailyMed; National Library of Medicine.

adagrasib.sourceNotes: Data extracted from FDA package insert (DailyMed) for adagrasib. No ASHP guidelines were provided or referenced.

monitoring.labs: ALT, AST, total bilirubin for hepatotoxicity monitoring
monitoring.vitals: ECG monitoring for QTc interval prolongation

clinicalAlerts: QTc interval prolongation: Mean QTcF change 18 ms at steady-state Cmax; withhold if QTc >500 ms or >60 ms increase from baseline
Hepatotoxicity: Monitor ALT, AST, and bilirubin; permanently discontinue if AST/ALT >3×ULN with total bilirubin >2×ULN
Interstitial Lung Disease/Pneumonitis: Permanently discontinue if confirmed
Gastrointestinal adverse reactions: Nausea, vomiting, diarrhea requiring supportive care and dose modifications
Strong CYP3A inhibitors increase adagrasib exposure (Cmax 2.4-fold, AUC 4-fold after single dose)
Strong CYP3A inducers decrease adagrasib exposure significantly (88% decrease in Cmax, 95% decrease in AUC)
Adagrasib significantly increases midazolam exposure (Cmax 4.8-fold, AUC 21-fold at 400 mg twice daily)

adalimumab.drugClass: Tumor necrosis factor (TNF) blocker; Recombinant human IgG1 monoclonal antibody
adalimumab.sourceNotes: Data extracted from FDA package insert for IDACIO (adalimumab-aacf) available on DailyMed; no ASHP guidelines specific to IV administration were provided as this drug is approved for subcutaneous use only.

administration.label: Route of Administration
administration.value: Subcutaneous injection only. Not approved for intravenous use.

clinicalAlerts: Adalimumab products are NOT approved for intravenous use. The pharmacokinetics were studied with intravenous doses ranging from 0.25 to 10 mg/kg for research purposes only.
This drug is formulated and approved exclusively for subcutaneous administration.
IV compatibility, reconstitution, dilution, and infusion parameters are not applicable to this subcutaneous-only medication.

adenosine.sourceNotes: Data extracted from FDA package insert via DailyMed; no ASHP guidelines available for adenosine IV administration.

dilution.label: Compatible Diluents
dilution.value: No dilution required; supplied as ready-to-use 3 mg/mL solution

dilution.label: Final Concentration
dilution.value: 3 mg/mL (as supplied)

administration.label: Route
administration.value: Rapid bolus intravenous injection

administration.label: Administration Rate
administration.value: Administer over 1 to 2 seconds as rapid bolus

administration.label: Administration Site
administration.value: Directly into vein or as close to patient as possible if given into IV line; followed by rapid saline flush to ensure solution reaches systemic circulation

administration.label: Central Venous Administration
administration.value: Has not been systematically studied; dose recommendations based on peripheral venous bolus dosing

ivPush.dilutionRequired: No dilution required; use as supplied
ivPush.specialConditions: Must be followed immediately by rapid saline flush. Administer as close to patient as possible if given into IV line to ensure systemic circulation.

monitoring.vitals: Electrocardiogram monitoring required; monitor for heart block, asystole, new arrhythmias at time of conversion
monitoring.duringInfusion: Continuous ECG monitoring during administration; observe for transient arrhythmias, heart block, asystole, and conversion rhythms; monitor respiratory status in patients with lung disease

sideEffects: Seizure activity including tonic clonic seizures
clinicalAlerts: May produce transient or prolonged asystole with fatal outcomes in some cases
Rarely ventricular fibrillation reported, especially with concomitant digoxin use or digoxin and verapamil combination
Use with caution in patients receiving digoxin or digoxin and verapamil
At conversion, 55% of patients develop new rhythms (PVCs, APCs, atrial fibrillation, sinus bradycardia/tachycardia, varying degrees of AV block); generally self-limiting
Avoid in patients with bronchoconstriction or bronchospasm (e.g., asthma); use with caution in obstructive lung disease
Effects antagonized by methylxanthines (caffeine, theophylline); larger doses may be required
Effects potentiated by dipyridamole; smaller doses may be effective
Carbamazepine may increase degree of heart block
Very short half-life (less than 10 seconds in whole blood); effects generally self-limiting

citation.primary: Adenosine injection prescribing information. DailyMed; FDA package insert.

ivCompatibility.value: Do not use

ivCompatibility.label: 5% Dextrose Injection, USP

ivCompatibility.label: Other drugs in same infusion bag or IV line
ivCompatibility.value: Do not combine with other drugs

monitoring.labs: Proteinuria (urine protein), AST, ALT, serum creatinine, complete blood count with differential (leukopenia, neutropenia, thrombocytopenia)
monitoring.vitals: Blood pressure for hypertension monitoring

sideEffects: Infections (urinary tract infection, nasopharyngitis, upper respiratory tract infection, pneumonia)
Venous thromboembolic events (deep venous thrombosis, pulmonary embolism)
clinicalAlerts: Severe hemorrhage
Gastrointestinal perforation
Impaired wound healing - temporarily suspend at least 4 weeks prior to elective surgery
Fistula formation
Hypertension - monitor blood pressure and suspend for uncontrolled hypertension
Hypertensive crisis or hypertensive encephalopathy - discontinue if occurs
Arterial thromboembolic events - discontinue if occurs
Proteinuria - suspend for proteinuria ≥2 grams per 24 hours
Nephrotic syndrome or thrombotic microangiopathy - discontinue if occurs
Neutropenia and neutropenic complications
Diarrhea and dehydration
Reversible posterior leukoencephalopathy syndrome (RPLS) - discontinue if occurs

alemtuzumab.drugClass: CD52-directed cytolytic antibody, humanized monoclonal antibody
alemtuzumab.sourceNotes: Data extracted from FDA package insert (DailyMed) for alemtuzumab; ASHP sources contained only general background and adverse effects without administration or monitoring details beyond the PI.

dilution.label: Compatible Diluents
dilution.value: 0.9% Sodium Chloride USP or 5% Dextrose in Water USP

dilution.label: Final Volume
dilution.value: 100 mL

dilution.label: 3 mg Dose Volume to Withdraw
dilution.value: 0.1 mL

dilution.label: 30 mg Dose Volume to Withdraw
dilution.value: 1 mL

dilution.label: Stability After Dilution
dilution.value: Use within 8 hours after dilution

dilution.label: Storage After Dilution
dilution.value: Room temperature 15°C to 30°C (59°F to 86°F) or refrigerated 2°C to 8°C (36°F to 46°F); protect from light

administration.label: Route
administration.value: Intravenous infusion

administration.label: Infusion Duration
administration.value: 2 hours

administration.label: Rate Restriction
administration.value: Do not administer as intravenous push or bolus

administration.label: Premedication
administration.value: Diphenhydramine 50 mg and acetaminophen 500–1000 mg administered 30 minutes prior to first infusion and each dose escalation

administration.label: Dose Escalation
administration.value: Administer 3 mg daily until infusion-related reactions ≤ Grade 2, then 10 mg daily until infusion-related reactions ≤ Grade 2, then 30 mg three times per week on alternate days

administration.label: Maintenance Dosing
administration.value: 30 mg/day three times per week on alternate days (e.g., Mon-Wed-Fri) for total duration of 12 weeks including dose escalation

administration.label: Maximum Single Dose
administration.value: 30 mg

administration.label: Maximum Cumulative Weekly Dose
administration.value: 90 mg

administration.label: Re-escalation Requirement
administration.value: If dosing held ≥7 days during treatment, re-initiate at 3 mg and escalate to 10 mg then 30 mg as tolerated

ivCompatibility.label: PVC Bags
ivCompatibility.value: Compatible

ivCompatibility.label: PVC Administration Sets
ivCompatibility.value: Compatible

ivCompatibility.label: Polyethylene-lined PVC Administration Sets
ivCompatibility.value: Compatible

ivCompatibility.label: Other Drug Substances
ivCompatibility.value: Do not add or simultaneously infuse other drug substances through the same intravenous line

monitoring.labs: Monitor for cytopenias including neutropenia (ANC), thrombocytopenia (platelet count), anemia; monitor for autoimmune idiopathic thrombocytopenia and autoimmune hemolytic anemia; CMV surveillance using PCR assay weekly from initiation through completion of therapy and every 2 weeks for first 2 months following therapy; monitor CD4+ count (continue prophylaxis until CD4+ count ≥200 cells/µL)
monitoring.vitals: Monitor heart rate (mean increase of 22 to 26 beats/min observed for at least 2 hours following initial infusion)
monitoring.duringInfusion: Monitor patients during infusion for infusion-related reactions including pyrexia, chills, hypotension, urticaria, nausea, rash, tachycardia, and dyspnea; withhold therapy for Grade 3 or 4 infusion-related reactions

sideEffects: Infusion-related reactions (pyrexia, chills, hypotension, urticaria, nausea, rash, tachycardia, dyspnea)
Infections (CMV viremia, CMV infection, bacterial, viral, fungal, protozoan infections)
Gastrointestinal symptoms (nausea, emesis, abdominal pain, diarrhea)
blackBoxWarnings: Cytopenias: Serious, including fatal, pancytopenia/marrow hypoplasia, autoimmune idiopathic thrombocytopenia, and autoimmune hemolytic anemia can occur. Single doses greater than 30 mg or cumulative doses greater than 90 mg per week increase the incidence of pancytopenia.

Infusion-Related Reactions: Serious, including fatal, infusion-related reactions can occur. Carefully monitor patients during infusions and withhold for Grade 3 or 4 infusion-related reactions. Gradually escalate to recommended dose at initiation or after interruption of therapy for 7 or more days.

Immunosuppression/Infections: Serious, including fatal, bacterial, viral, fungal, and protozoan infections can occur. Administer prophylaxis against Pneumocystis jirovecii pneumonia (PCP) and herpes virus infections.
clinicalAlerts: Administer trimethoprim/sulfamethoxazole DS twice daily 3 times per week (or equivalent) as PCP prophylaxis
Administer famciclovir 250 mg BID or equivalent as herpes viral prophylaxis
Continue PCP and herpes viral prophylaxis for minimum of 2 months after completion of therapy or until CD4+ count ≥200 cells/µL, whichever occurs later
Withhold therapy during serious infection or other serious adverse reactions until resolution
Discontinue for autoimmune anemia or autoimmune thrombocytopenia
Severe lymphopenia with rapid and sustained decrease in lymphocyte subsets occurs following administration
Greatest occurrence of infusion-related reactions during initial week of treatment; decreases with subsequent doses
Single-dose vial contains no preservatives; discard vial including any unused portion after withdrawal of dose
Do not shake vial

citation.primary: CAMPATH (alemtuzumab) injection package insert. Genzyme Corporation.

alprostadil.sourceNotes: Data extracted from FDA package insert for PROSTIN VR PEDIATRIC (alprostadil) available via DailyMed; ASHP reference did not contain relevant IV administration guidance for this indication.

dilution.label: Compatible Diluents
dilution.value: Sodium Chloride Injection USP or Dextrose Injection USP

dilution.label: Preparation Method
dilution.value: Add appropriate volume of IV solution to volumetric chamber first, then add undiluted alprostadil to the solution, avoiding direct contact with chamber walls

dilution.label: Concentration Range
dilution.value: 2 to 20 micrograms/mL (1 ampoule [500 mcg] diluted in 25 to 250 mL)

dilution.label: Stability
dilution.value: Prepare fresh infusion solutions every 24 hours; discard any solution more than 24 hours old

dilution.label: Special Handling
dilution.value: Undiluted solution may interact with plastic sidewalls of volumetric chambers causing hazy appearance; replace solution and chamber if this occurs

administration.label: Route
administration.value: Continuous intravenous infusion into a large vein, or through umbilical artery catheter placed at ductal opening

administration.label: Starting Dose
administration.value: 0.05 to 0.1 micrograms/kg/min (0.1 micrograms/kg/min is recommended starting dose)

administration.label: Dose Adjustment
administration.value: After therapeutic response, reduce to lowest effective dose: 0.1 to 0.05 to 0.025 to 0.01 micrograms/kg/min

administration.label: Maximum Dose
administration.value: Up to 0.4 micrograms/kg/min (higher rates generally do not produce greater effects)

administration.label: Duration
administration.value: Continuous infusion; use shortest time and lowest dose to achieve desired effect

administration.label: Administration Notes
administration.value: Must be diluted before administration; infuse for shortest time at lowest effective dose due to risk of antral hyperplasia with prolonged use (>120 hours)

ivCompatibility.label: Sodium Chloride Injection USP
ivCompatibility.value: Compatible for dilution

ivCompatibility.label: Dextrose Injection USP
ivCompatibility.value: Compatible for dilution

monitoring.labs: Blood oxygenation (pO2) in infants with restricted pulmonary blood flow; systemic blood pressure and blood pH in infants with restricted systemic blood flow
monitoring.vitals: Arterial pressure should be monitored intermittently by umbilical artery catheter, auscultation, or Doppler transducer; decrease infusion rate immediately if arterial pressure falls significantly
monitoring.duringInfusion: Respiratory status should be monitored throughout treatment; ventilatory assistance must be immediately available due to risk of apnea (10-12% incidence, most often in neonates <2 kg, usually within first hour)

sideEffects: Flushing (10%, more common after intraarterial dosing)
Cortical proliferation of long bones (with long-term infusion)
blackBoxWarnings: Apnea is experienced by about 10 to 12% of neonates with congenital heart defects treated with PROSTIN VR PEDIATRIC. Apnea is most often seen in neonates weighing less than 2 kg at birth and usually appears during the first hour of drug infusion. Therefore, respiratory status should be monitored throughout treatment, and PROSTIN VR PEDIATRIC should be used where ventilatory assistance is immediately available.
clinicalAlerts: Gastric outlet obstruction secondary to antral hyperplasia may occur; appears related to duration of therapy and cumulative dose; neonates receiving drug for >120 hours should be closely monitored
Contains 79% ethanol which may cause CNS depressant effects; co-administration with medicines containing propylene glycol or ethanol may lead to accumulation
Use cautiously in neonates with bleeding tendencies as alprostadil inhibits platelet aggregation
Should not be used in neonates with respiratory distress syndrome; differential diagnosis required between hyaline membrane disease and cyanotic heart disease

Tissue sections have shown intimal lacerations, decreased medial muscularity, disruption of elastic lamina, localized dilatations, and vessel wall edema in ductus and pulmonary arteries of infants treated 10 hours to 12 days

alteplase.sourceNotes: Data extracted from FDA package insert (DailyMed) for Cathflo Activase (alteplase) for intracatheter instillation; ASHP articles referenced tenecteplase, not alteplase IV administration.

reconstitution.label: Diluent
reconstitution.value: Sterile Water for Injection, USP (do not use Bacteriostatic Water for Injection)

reconstitution.label: Mixing Instructions
reconstitution.value: Gently swirl until contents completely dissolved. DO NOT SHAKE. Slight foaming not unusual; allow bubbles to dissipate.

reconstitution.label: Reconstituted Appearance
reconstitution.value: Colorless to pale yellow transparent solution

reconstitution.label: Stability After Reconstitution
reconstitution.value: May be used for intracatheter instillation within 8 hours when stored at 2–30°C (36–86°F)

administration.label: Route
administration.value: Intracatheter instillation for restoration of function to central venous access devices

administration.label: Dose ≥30 kg
administration.value: 2 mg in 2 mL

administration.label: Dose <30 kg
administration.value: 110% of internal lumen volume of catheter, not to exceed 2 mg in 2 mL

administration.label: Administration Technique
administration.value: Instill appropriate dose into occluded catheter; avoid excessive pressure to prevent catheter rupture or clot expulsion

administration.label: Dwell Time
administration.value: Assess function after 30 minutes; if not functional, reassess after 120 minutes

administration.label: Second Dose
administration.value: If catheter function not restored at 120 minutes, a second dose of equal amount may be instilled

administration.label: Post-Instillation
administration.value: After function restored, aspirate 4–5 mL blood (≥10 kg patients) or 3 mL (<10 kg patients) to remove alteplase and residual clot; gently irrigate catheter with 0.9% Sodium Chloride

administration.label: Precaution
administration.value: Avoid vigorous suction to prevent vascular wall damage or soft-walled catheter collapse

ivCompatibility.label: Other Medications
ivCompatibility.value: No other medication should be added to solutions containing Cathflo Activase

monitoring.duringInfusion: Monitor for signs of bleeding, hypersensitivity (urticaria, angioedema, anaphylaxis), and signs of infection release into systemic circulation

sideEffects: Major hemorrhage (severe blood loss >5 mL/kg, requiring transfusion, or causing hypotension)
Subclavian and upper extremity deep venous thrombosis
clinicalAlerts: Death and permanent disability reported in patients experiencing stroke and serious bleeding when receiving pharmacologic doses of thrombolytic
Use with caution in presence of known or suspected catheter infection; may release localized infection into systemic circulation
Catheter dysfunction may be due to conditions other than thrombus (malposition, mechanical failure, suture constriction, lipid deposits, drug precipitates)
Caution in patients with active internal bleeding or recent (within 48 hours) surgery, obstetrical delivery, percutaneous biopsy, or puncture of non-compressible vessels
Caution in patients with thrombocytopenia, hemostatic defects, severe hepatic or renal disease, or high embolic risk
If serious bleeding in critical location occurs, stop treatment and withdraw drug from catheter
Contains no antibacterial preservatives; reconstitute immediately before use

citation.primary: Cathflo Activase (alteplase) [package insert]. South San Francisco, CA: Genentech, Inc.

amantadine.sourceNotes: Data extracted from FDA package insert via DailyMed for amantadine hydrochloride; ASHP source provided was not relevant to this drug.

monitoring.drugLevels: Plasma amantadine concentrations may be monitored; toxicity appears more prevalent as concentration increases
monitoring.vitals: Monitor for orthostatic hypotension, hypertension, tachycardia

clinicalAlerts: Deaths have been reported from overdose with amantadine; lowest reported acute lethal dose was 1 gram
Deaths due to drug accumulation have been reported in patients with renal impairment prescribed higher than recommended doses
Suicide attempts, some fatal, have been reported in patients treated with amantadine
Increased seizure activity may occur in patients with history of epilepsy or seizures
Corneal edema has been reported; can be bilateral with onset from weeks to years after starting; may require corneal grafts if not recognized
Patients with congestive heart failure or peripheral edema should be followed closely
Contraindicated in patients with untreated angle closure glaucoma due to anticholinergic effects
Abrupt discontinuation may precipitate delirium, agitation, delusions, hallucinations, paranoid reaction, stupor, anxiety, depression and slurred speech

In difficult and complicated infections beyond 10 days, amikacin serum levels and renal, auditory, and vestibular functions should be monitored
Dosage adjustment required in patients with impaired renal function

aminocaproic acid.sourceNotes: Data extracted from FDA package insert (DailyMed label dated May 2020); ASHP source discusses cardiac surgery applications but provides no IV administration guidance beyond PI scope.

administration.label: Maintenance Infusion

administration.label: Rate Restriction
administration.value: RAPID INJECTION UNDILUTED INTO A VEIN IS NOT RECOMMENDED; rapid IV administration may induce hypotension, bradycardia, and/or arrhythmia

administration.label: Infusion Precautions
administration.value: Strict attention to proper needle insertion and position to guard against thrombophlebitis

ivCompatibility.value: Compatible

ivCompatibility.value: Should not be administered together; increased thrombosis risk

ivCompatibility.label: Anti-Inhibitor Coagulant Concentrates
ivCompatibility.value: Should not be administered together; increased thrombosis risk

monitoring.labs: CPK levels should be monitored in patients on long-term therapy (stop if CPK rises); fibrinolysis tests (clot lysis, profibrinolysin, fibrinolysin, antifibrinolysin); platelet count if concerned for DIC; protamine paracoagulation test; euglobulin clot lysis test; BUN
monitoring.drugLevels: Therapeutic concentration to maintain fibrinolysis inhibition is 0.99 mMol/L or 0.13 mg/mL; higher concentrations may occur in severe renal failure
monitoring.vitals: Monitor for hypotension, bradycardia, arrhythmia during infusion
monitoring.duringInfusion: Monitor for hypersensitivity reactions; observe infusion site for thrombophlebitis, pain, and necrosis

sideEffects: Pain and necrosis at injection site
clinicalAlerts: Contains benzyl alcohol 0.9% as preservative; associated with fatal 'Gasping Syndrome' in premature neonates - DO NOT use in neonates or low-birth weight infants
Contraindicated when active intravascular clotting process is present
Must differentiate primary fibrinolysis from DIC before administration; must not be used in DIC without concomitant heparin

May cause intrarenal obstruction (glomerular capillary thrombosis or clots in renal pelvis/ureters) in upper urinary tract bleeding - should not be used in hematuria of upper urinary tract origin unless benefits outweigh risks
Skeletal muscle weakness with rhabdomyolysis and acute renal failure reported with prolonged use - stop if CPK rises
Cardiac muscle damage and myocardial fatty degeneration reported in animal studies
Increased incidence of neurological deficits (hydrocephalus, cerebral ischemia, cerebral vasospasm) reported with antifibrinolytic use in subarachnoid hemorrhage
Template bleeding time prolongation reported with continuous IV infusion >24 g/day
Higher plasma concentrations may occur in severe renal failure

aminophylline.sourceNotes: Primary source: FDA package insert (DailyMed); ASHP source supplemented hazard monitoring data for therapeutic drug level monitoring in geriatric patients.

dilution.label: Compatible Diluents
dilution.value: Administered by diluted intravenous infusion (specific diluents not detailed in PI)

dilution.label: Administration Forms
dilution.value: Slow intravenous injection or diluted and administered by intravenous infusion

dilution.label: Stability
dilution.value: Single-dose injection; unused portion should be discarded when smaller doses are required

administration.label: Route
administration.value: Intravenous

administration.label: Administration Method
administration.value: Slow intravenous injection or intravenous infusion

administration.label: Loading Dose Infusion Time
administration.value: Loading dose of 4.6 mg/kg theophylline (5.7 mg/kg as aminophylline) administered over 30 minutes

administration.label: Maintenance Infusion Rate - Adults
administration.value: 0.4 mg/kg/hr theophylline (0.5 mg/kg/hr as aminophylline) constant intravenous infusion

administration.label: Maintenance Infusion Rate - Children (1-9 years)
administration.value: 0.8 mg/kg/hr theophylline (1.0 mg/kg/hr as aminophylline) constant intravenous infusion

administration.label: Special Precautions
administration.value: Small dose increments recommended due to nonlinearity of elimination; dose adjustments based on ideal body weight

monitoring.labs: Serum theophylline concentrations; more frequent measurements during initiation of therapy and in conditions that may significantly alter theophylline clearance. In patients with reduced protein binding, measurement of unbound serum theophylline concentration recommended (target unbound concentration 6-12 mcg/mL).
monitoring.drugLevels: Therapeutic range: 10-15 mcg/mL (average serum concentration). Bronchodilation occurs at 5-20 mcg/mL; clinically important improvement typically requires >10 mcg/mL. Increased adverse reactions at >20 mcg/mL. In acutely ill patients: measure at 24-hour intervals. ASHP data identifies aminophylline/theophylline as associated with hazard factors ≥2.0 for ADRs and therapeutic errors in older adults, warranting enhanced monitoring.

clinicalAlerts: Nonlinear pharmacokinetics: dose-dependent metabolism may begin at serum concentrations <10 mcg/mL in some patients; small dose increments advised
Wide intersubject variability in theophylline metabolism; pharmacokinetics cannot be predicted by age, sex, body weight or demographic characteristics
Frequent serum concentration monitoring required in acutely ill patients (at least every 24 hours)
Geriatric patients: 30% decrease in clearance compared to young adults; careful dose reduction and frequent monitoring required
Neonates: very low clearance, 50% excreted unchanged in urine; dose reduction and frequent monitoring required in neonates with reduced renal function
Reduced protein binding in certain populations (premature neonates, hepatic cirrhosis, acidemia, elderly, third trimester pregnancy) may cause toxicity at therapeutic total serum concentrations
Multiple drug interactions and concurrent illnesses significantly alter pharmacokinetics

citation.primary: Aminophylline Injection, USP [package insert]. DailyMed, National Library of Medicine.
citation.ashp: Cobaugh DJ, Krenzelok EP. Adverse drug reactions and therapeutic errors in older adults: a hazard factor analysis of poison center data. Am J Health Syst Pharm. 2006 Nov 15;63(22):2228-34.

monitoring.duringInfusion: Monitor infusion site — extravasation of hypertonic TPN causes tissue necrosis

Central line required for hypertonic formulations

Monitor glucose closely — insulin may be needed
Strict aseptic technique required — TPN supports microbial growth

amiodarone.sourceNotes: Data extracted from FDA package insert via DailyMed; ASHP sources reviewed but did not contain relevant IV administration or monitoring data for this drug.

dilution.label: First Rapid Loading (150 mg over 10 min)

dilution.value: Adsorbs to PVC tubing; all clinical experience has been with PVC tubing

dilution.label: In-line Filter
dilution.value: Use an in-line filter during administration

administration.value: 150 mg over 10 minutes (15 mg/min); infuse 100 mL at rate of 10 mL/min

administration.label: First 24 Hours - Slow Loading
administration.value: 360 mg over next 6 hours (1 mg/min); infuse 200 mL at rate of 0.556 mL/min

administration.label: First 24 Hours - Maintenance
administration.value: 540 mg over remaining 18 hours (0.5 mg/min); rate of 0.278 mL/min

administration.label: After First 24 Hours - Maintenance
administration.value: 0.5 mg/min (720 mg per 24 hours); may be increased to achieve effective arrhythmia suppression

administration.label: Supplemental Dose for Breakthrough VF/VT
administration.value: 150 mg in 100 mL D5W infused over 10 minutes

administration.label: Maximum Initial Infusion Rate
administration.value: Do not exceed 30 mg/min

administration.label: Duration of Maintenance
administration.value: Maintenance infusion up to 0.5 mg/min can be continued for 2 to 3 weeks; limited experience beyond 3 weeks

administration.label: Route
administration.value: Intravenous via central venous catheter (preferred); use volumetric infusion pump

administration.label: Warning on Drop Counter Sets
administration.value: Do not use drop counter infusion sets; may result in underdosage up to 30%

administration.label: High Dose Warning
administration.value: Loading infusions at much higher concentrations and faster rates than recommended have resulted in hepatocellular necrosis and acute renal failure leading to death

ivCompatibility.label: Dextrose 5% in Water (D5W)
ivCompatibility.value: Compatible diluent for all amiodarone infusions

ivCompatibility.label: PVC Tubing
ivCompatibility.value: Amiodarone adsorbs to PVC tubing; all clinical experience has been with PVC tubing

ivCompatibility.label: Evacuated Glass Containers
ivCompatibility.value: Do not use for admixing; incompatibility with buffer in container may cause precipitation

monitoring.drugLevels: Peak concentrations after 10-minute infusions of 150 mg range between 7 and 26 mg/L; after 48 hours of continued infusions, mean serum concentrations between 0.7 to 1.4 mg/L observed
monitoring.vitals: Close clinical monitoring for hypotension; treatment-emergent, drug-related hypotension occurred in 16% of patients
monitoring.duringInfusion: Monitor for hypotension during infusion; minimize potential for hypotension by infusing supplemental doses over 10 minutes

sideEffects: Peripheral vein phlebitis (high incidence with concentrations >3 mg/mL)
Hepatocellular necrosis (with loading infusions at much higher concentrations than recommended)
Acute renal failure (with loading infusions at much higher concentrations than recommended)
clinicalAlerts: Intravenous amiodarone loading infusions at much higher concentrations and rates of infusion much faster than recommended have resulted in hepatocellular necrosis and acute renal failure, leading to death
Mean daily doses above 2100 mg in first 24 hours were associated with increased risk of hypotension
Concentrations greater than 3 mg/mL in D5W have been associated with high incidence of peripheral vein phlebitis
Use drop counter infusion sets may result in underdosage up to 30%; must use volumetric infusion pump
Contains polysorbate 80 which leaches DEHP from PVC
Administer through central venous catheter whenever possible

clinicalAlerts: Anaphylaxis has been reported with amphotericin B deoxycholate and other amphotericin B-containing drugs, including amphotericin B liposome for injection
If severe anaphylactic reaction occurs, infusion should be immediately discontinued and patient should not receive further infusions
Contraindicated in patients with known hypersensitivity to amphotericin B deoxycholate or any product constituents unless benefit outweighs risk
Not interchangeable with other amphotericin B products on a mg per mg basis; different products not equivalent in pharmacodynamics, pharmacokinetics, and dosing

administration.value: intravenous infusion over 15-30 minutes

administration.value: deep intramuscular injection

monitoring.labs: hepatic function should be monitored at regular intervals in patients with hepatic impairment

sideEffects: Increased AST, ALT, alkaline phosphatase, and LDH
Decreased hemoglobin, hematocrit, RBC, WBC, neutrophils, lymphocytes, platelets
Increased lymphocytes, monocytes, basophils, eosinophils, and platelets
Decreased serum albumin and total proteins
Presence of RBCs and hyaline casts in urine
clinicalAlerts: Serious and occasionally fatal hypersensitivity (anaphylactic) reactions have been reported in patients on penicillin therapy
Hepatic dysfunction, including hepatitis and cholestatic jaundice, has been associated with use; deaths have been reported

May cause severe skin reactions such as toxic epidermal necrolysis (TEN), Stevens-Johnson syndrome (SJS), dermatitis exfoliative, erythema multiforme, and acute generalized exanthematous pustulosis (AGEP)
Clostridium difficile-associated diarrhea (CDAD) has been reported with use and may range in severity from mild diarrhea to fatal colitis
Contraindicated in individuals with history of serious hypersensitivity reactions (e.g., anaphylaxis or Stevens-Johnson syndrome) to ampicillin, sulbactam, or other beta-lactam antibacterial drugs
Contraindicated in patients with previous history of cholestatic jaundice/hepatic dysfunction associated with ampicillin and sulbactam

Average time to death was 15 days after treatment in clinical trials

Prepared solutions should not be exposed to direct sunlight.

monitoring.labs: aPTT (activated partial thromboplastin time), ACT (activated clotting time), PT (prothrombin time), INR (International Normalized Ratio), thrombin time (TT), platelet count for HIT monitoring
monitoring.drugLevels: Steady-state plasma argatroban concentrations correlate with anticoagulant effects; therapeutic monitoring via aPTT or ACT recommended
monitoring.duringInfusion: Anticoagulant effects produced immediately upon initiation; steady-state attained within 1 to 3 hours

clinicalAlerts: Dosage should be decreased in patients with hepatic impairment (decreased clearance and increased elimination half-life)
All forms of heparin therapy should be immediately discontinued when HIT is suspected
Co-administration with warfarin produces combined effect on INR measurement; relationship dependent on argatroban dose and thromboplastin reagent ISI
For argatroban doses greater than 2 mcg/kg/min, INR on warfarin alone cannot be reliably predicted from combined therapy INR
Use of low-molecular-weight heparins or initiation of warfarin without additional effective anticoagulation is not recommended in presence of HIT

arsenic trioxide.sourceNotes: Data extracted from FDA package insert for TRISENOX (arsenic trioxide) accessed via DailyMed.

dilution.label: Compatible Diluents
dilution.value: 5% Dextrose Injection, USP or 0.9% Sodium Chloride Injection, USP

dilution.label: Stability After Dilution
dilution.value: Diluted solution is stable for 24 hours at room temperature and 48 hours when refrigerated

dilution.label: Preparation Notes
dilution.value: TRISENOX is supplied as a ready-to-use 2 mg/mL solution in 10 mL single-dose vials (12 mg total). Withdraw appropriate dose and dilute immediately prior to administration.

administration.label: Route
administration.value: Intravenous infusion

administration.label: Infusion Duration
administration.value: Infuse over 1-2 hours; infusion may be extended up to 4 hours if acute vasomotor reactions occur

administration.label: Administration Notes
administration.value: Do not mix with other medications. A separate dedicated line is not required.

monitoring.labs: During induction: monitor coagulation studies, blood counts, and chemistries at least 2-3 times per week through recovery. During consolidation: monitor coagulation studies, blood counts, and chemistries at least weekly. Monitor electrolytes (potassium, calcium, magnesium) and correct abnormalities prior to administration.
monitoring.vitals: Monitor blood pressure; hypotension may occur with differentiation syndrome
monitoring.duringInfusion: Monitor for acute vasomotor reactions. Monitor ECG at baseline, at least weekly during induction, and at least every other week during consolidation for QTc prolongation.

sideEffects: Hepatotoxicity (elevated AST, ALT, bilirubin, alkaline phosphatase)
blackBoxWarnings: Differentiation syndrome: Can be fatal if not treated. Monitor for fever, dyspnea, weight gain, pulmonary infiltrates, and pleural or pericardial effusions. Withhold TRISENOX and administer high-dose corticosteroids at first suspicion.
QTc prolongation: Can lead to torsade de pointes or complete AV block. Monitor ECG, correct electrolyte abnormalities, and avoid concomitant QTc-prolonging drugs.
Encephalopathy and Wernicke's encephalopathy: Can be severe and fatal in thiamine-deficient patients. Assess thiamine status before and during treatment; supplement as needed.
clinicalAlerts: Differentiation syndrome prophylaxis with prednisone 0.5 mg/kg daily from day 1 until end of induction is recommended in newly-diagnosed low-risk APL.
Correct electrolyte abnormalities (potassium, calcium, magnesium) before initiating therapy and monitor closely during treatment.
Withhold TRISENOX if QTc exceeds 500 msec; resume at reduced dose after correction of electrolytes and QTc normalization.
Administer hydroxyurea if WBC count exceeds 10 Gi/L to manage leukocytosis.
Monitor for hepatotoxicity and modify dose or discontinue if severe elevations in liver enzymes or bilirubin occur.

citation.primary: TRISENOX (arsenic trioxide) injection [package insert]. North Wales, PA: Teva Pharmaceuticals USA, Inc.

Acute oxalate nephropathy (with prolonged high doses)
Chronic oxalate nephropathy (with prolonged high doses)
Severe hemolysis (in patients with glucose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase deficiency)
clinicalAlerts: Oxalate nephropathy and nephrolithiasis risk with prolonged administration of high doses
Hemolysis risk in patients with glucose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase deficiency
Pregnant women, lactating women, and patients with G6PD deficiency should not exceed U.S. RDA or daily Adequate Intake level
Maximum recommended treatment duration is 7 days; repeat dosing not recommended in pediatric patients <11 years
Pharmacy Bulk Package: for single-entry dispensing only; complete all dispensing within 4 hours of vial entry

Contraindicated in patients with history of serious hemorrhagic events during previous asparaginase therapy

administration.value: Good or excellent intubation conditions in 2 to 2.5 minutes in most patients

administration.label: Time to Maximum Block
administration.value: Maximum neuromuscular block achieved approximately 3 to 5 minutes after injection

administration.label: Duration of Block
administration.value: Clinically required neuromuscular block generally lasts 20 to 35 minutes under balanced anesthesia

administration.label: Maintenance Dose
administration.value: 0.08 to 0.1 mg/kg; first maintenance dose generally required 20 to 45 minutes after initial injection, subsequent doses at approximately 15 to 25 minute intervals under balanced anesthesia

administration.label: Continuous Infusion
administration.value: Initiated only after initial bolus dose (0.3 to 0.5 mg/kg). Rate adjusted according to patient response as determined by peripheral nerve stimulation. Use precision infusion device for accurate dosing.

administration.label: Administration Timing
administration.value: Should not be administered before unconsciousness has been induced

administration.label: Dose Adjustment with Inhalation Anesthetics
administration.value: With isoflurane or enflurane: reduce initial dose by approximately one-third (to 0.25 to 0.35 mg/kg) if administered under steady-state anesthesia. With halothane: smaller dosage reductions may be considered (approximately 20% potentiating effect).

administration.label: Special Populations - Cardiovascular Disease
administration.value: 0.3 to 0.4 mg/kg given slowly or in divided doses over one minute

administration.label: Pediatric (≥2 years)
administration.value: No dosage adjustments required; same as adult dosing

administration.label: Infants (1 month to 2 years)
administration.value: 0.3 to 0.4 mg/kg under halothane anesthesia

administration.label: Following Succinylcholine Use
administration.value: 0.3 to 0.4 mg/kg for adults under balanced anesthesia. Patient should be permitted to recover from succinylcholine effects prior to atracurium administration.

ivCompatibility.label: Alkaline Solutions
ivCompatibility.value: Should not be mixed in same syringe or administered simultaneously through same needle with alkaline solutions (e.g., barbiturate solutions)

ivPush.pushRate: Given slowly or in divided doses over one minute for patients with significant cardiovascular disease or greater risk of histamine release
ivPush.dilutionRequired: Not required for bolus administration; supplied solution may be given as intravenous bolus injection
ivPush.specialConditions: Should not be administered before unconsciousness has been induced. Use slower administration (over one minute or in divided doses) in patients with significant cardiovascular disease, history suggesting greater risk of histamine release (severe anaphylactoid reactions or asthma), neuromuscular disease, severe electrolyte disorders, or carcinomatosis.

monitoring.vitals: Hemodynamic changes are minimal within recommended dose range; monitor for histamine release effects including falls in blood pressure, especially with doses ≥0.6 mg/kg
monitoring.duringInfusion: Use of peripheral nerve stimulator is recommended to assess degree of muscle relaxation, permit most advantageous use of atracurium, minimize possibility of overdosage or underdosage, and assist in evaluation of recovery. Monitor muscle twitch response. ASHP guidelines recommend train-of-four (TOF) stimulation or double-burst stimulation (DBS) to monitor neuromuscular block; TOF ratio should recover to ≥0.9 before reversal considered adequate.

sideEffects: Histamine release (minimal with doses up to 0.5 mg/kg; moderate with 0.6 mg/kg)
Falls in blood pressure (significant with 0.6 mg/kg doses)
Rare instances of breathing difficulties possibly related to incomplete reversal
clinicalAlerts: Should be administered only after patient recovers from succinylcholine-induced neuromuscular block
Duration of neuromuscular block does NOT correlate with plasma pseudocholinesterase levels and is NOT altered by absence of renal function

Possibility of substantial histamine release in sensitive individuals or in patients in whom substantial histamine release would be especially hazardous (e.g., patients with significant cardiovascular disease) must be considered

Slowly loses potency at rate of approximately 6% per year under refrigeration (5°C); approximately 5% per month at 25°C. Upon removal from refrigeration to room temperature, use within 14 days even if re-refrigerated
Reversal of neuromuscular block can be achieved with anticholinesterase agents (neostigmine, edrophonium, or pyridostigmine) in conjunction with anticholinergic agents (atropine or glycopyrrolate)
Tendency for residual neuromuscular block is increased if reversal is attempted at deep levels of block or if inadequate doses of reversal agents are employed

ASHP guidelines indicate postoperative residual neuromuscular block is frequent, dangerous, and difficult to recognize clinically; action of neuromuscular blocking agents should always be reversed unless there is unequivocal evidence of recovery

citation.primary: Atracurium Besylate Injection, USP Package Insert
citation.ashp: Bevan DR. Monitoring and reversal of neuromuscular block. Am J Health Syst Pharm. 1999 Jun 1;56(11 Suppl 1):S10-3.

atropine.sourceNotes: Primary source is FDA package insert via DailyMed; ASHP guideline on bradyarrhythmias supplemented monitoring considerations.

administration.label: Route
administration.value: Intravenous injection

administration.label: Rate
administration.value: Titrate based on heart rate, PR interval, blood pressure and symptoms

administration.label: Preparation
administration.value: Single-dose vial only; discard unused portion

administration.label: Visual inspection
administration.value: Do not administer unless solution is clear and seal is intact

ivPush.pushRate: Titrate based on heart rate, PR interval, blood pressure and symptoms
ivPush.dilutionRequired: Supplied as ready-to-use injection; 0.4 mg/mL or 1 mg/mL concentrations available
ivPush.specialConditions: In patients with coronary artery disease, limit total dose to 0.03 to 0.04 mg/kg

monitoring.vitals: Heart rate, PR interval, blood pressure
monitoring.duringInfusion: Titrate based on heart rate, PR interval, blood pressure and symptoms

sideEffects: Difficulty in micturition (may occur in elderly patients)
Skin rashes (in some instances progressed to exfoliation)
clinicalAlerts: Atropine-induced parasympathetic inhibition may be preceded by a transient phase of stimulation, especially on the heart where small doses first slow the rate before characteristic tachycardia develops
In patients with coronary artery disease, limit the total dose of atropine sulfate to 0.03 to 0.04 mg/kg
Occasionally a large dose may cause atrioventricular (A-V) block and nodal rhythm
Large or repeated doses may depress respiration
Atropine readily crosses the placental barrier and enters the fetal circulation

citation.primary: Atropine Sulfate Injection, USP Package Insert. HF Acquisition Co LLC, DBA HealthFirst.
citation.ashp: Ray L, Geier C, DeWitt KM. Pathophysiology and treatment of adults with arrhythmias in the emergency department, part 2: Ventricular and bradyarrhythmias. Am J Health Syst Pharm. 2023 Aug 18;80(17):1123-1136.

avatrombopag.sourceNotes: Data extracted from FDA package insert (DailyMed) for DOPTELET (avatrombopag). ASHP guideline mentions avatrombopag only as a therapeutic option for autoimmune thrombocytopenia but provides no IV-specific administration information.

monitoring.labs: Obtain platelet count prior to administration and on the day of procedure (chronic liver disease); assess platelet counts weekly until stable count ≥50×10⁹/L achieved, then monthly thereafter (chronic ITP); obtain platelet counts weekly for at least 4 weeks following discontinuation

clinicalAlerts: DOPTELET tablets and DOPTELET SPRINKLE are not substitutable on a mg-to-mg basis
DOPTELET SPRINKLE capsules should be opened and contents mixed with soft food or liquid; do not swallow capsules whole
Administer DOPTELET tablets and DOPTELET SPRINKLE with food to reduce variability of exposure
Do not use DOPTELET to normalize platelet counts
If switching between tablet and sprinkle formulations, monitor platelet counts weekly until stable

citation.primary: DOPTELET (avatrombopag) [package insert]. DailyMed, National Library of Medicine.
citation.ashp: Chung C, Allen E, Umoru G. Paraneoplastic syndromes: A focus on pathophysiology and supportive care. Am J Health Syst Pharm. 2022 Nov 7;79(22):1988-2000.

administration.label: Frequency
administration.value: Every 2 weeks

ivCompatibility.label: 0.9% Sodium Chloride Injection
ivCompatibility.value: Compatible for dilution

ivCompatibility.label: 0.45% Sodium Chloride Injection
ivCompatibility.value: Compatible for dilution

ivCompatibility.label: Other drugs via same IV line
ivCompatibility.value: Do not co-administer other drugs through the same intravenous line

monitoring.labs: Liver enzymes (ALT, AST), total bilirubin, blood creatinine
monitoring.duringInfusion: Monitor for infusion-related reactions

clinicalAlerts: Severe and fatal immune-mediated adverse reactions including pneumonitis, colitis, hepatitis, endocrinopathies, nephritis with renal dysfunction, exfoliative dermatologic conditions, myocarditis, and neurological toxicities
Infusion-related reactions - premedicate with antihistamine and acetaminophen for first 4 infusions and subsequently as needed
Complications of allogeneic HSCT
Major adverse cardiovascular events
Withhold for Grade 3 immune-mediated adverse reactions; permanently discontinue for Grade 4 or recurrent Grade 3 reactions
Interrupt or slow infusion rate for Grade 1 or 2 infusion reactions; permanently discontinue for Grade 3 or 4 infusion reactions

axicabtagene ciloleucel.drugClass: CD19-directed genetically modified autologous T cell immunotherapy (CAR T-cell therapy)
axicabtagene ciloleucel.sourceNotes: All fields derived from FDA package insert (DailyMed setid 9b70606e-b99c-4272-a0f1-b5523cce0c59); no ASHP data used.

reconstitution.label: Product Form
reconstitution.value: Supplied as frozen suspension in patient-specific infusion bag; no reconstitution required

reconstitution.label: Thawing Method
reconstitution.value: Thaw at approximately 37°C using water bath or dry thaw method until no visible ice in bag

reconstitution.label: Handling After Thaw
reconstitution.value: Gently mix contents to disperse clumps; do NOT wash, spin down, or re-suspend in new medium

reconstitution.label: Stability After Thaw
reconstitution.value: May be stored at room temperature (20°C to 25°C) for up to 3 hours

dilution.label: Dilution Required
dilution.value: None - infuse entire contents of bag undiluted

dilution.label: Priming Solution
dilution.value: Prime tubing with normal saline prior to infusion

dilution.label: Rinse Solution
dilution.value: Rinse tubing with normal saline after infusion to ensure all product delivered

administration.label: Route
administration.value: Intravenous only; for autologous use only

administration.label: Volume
administration.value: Approximately 68 mL per infusion bag

administration.label: Infusion Duration
administration.value: Infuse entire contents within 30 minutes

administration.label: Infusion Method
administration.value: By gravity or peristaltic pump; gently agitate product bag during infusion to prevent cell clumping

administration.label: Venous Access
administration.value: Central venous access recommended

administration.label: Filter Restriction
administration.value: Do NOT use a leukodepleting filter

administration.label: Patient Verification
administration.value: Verify patient identity matches identifiers on cassette and infusion bag before administration

administration.label: Pre-infusion Requirements
administration.value: Confirm tocilizumab and emergency equipment available prior to infusion

ivCompatibility.label: Normal Saline
ivCompatibility.value: Used for tubing prime and rinse

monitoring.labs: B-cell levels (expected B-cell aplasia period post-infusion); anti-CD19 CAR T cell levels (peak within 7-14 days)
monitoring.drugLevels: Anti-CD19 CAR T cells measurable in blood; median peak occurs within first 7-14 days; levels decline to near baseline by 3 months
monitoring.vitals: Monitor at least daily for 7 days following infusion for signs and symptoms of CRS and neurologic toxicities; continuous cardiac telemetry and pulse oximetry for Grade 2 or higher CRS
monitoring.duringInfusion: Monitor for signs of CRS and neurologic toxicities; gently agitate product bag during infusion

clinicalAlerts: Monitor patients at least daily for 7 days following infusion
Patients must remain within proximity of healthcare facility for at least 2 weeks following infusion
Advise patients to avoid driving for at least 2 weeks following infusion
Confirm availability of tocilizumab prior to infusion
For Grade 2 or higher CRS, monitor with continuous cardiac telemetry and pulse oximetry
Follow universal precautions and local biosafety guidelines for handling and disposal

citation.primary: YESCARTA (axicabtagene ciloleucel) [package insert]. Kite Pharma, Inc.

azacitidine.drugClass: nucleoside metabolic inhibitor; pyrimidine nucleoside analog
azacitidine.sourceNotes: Data extracted from FDA package insert accessed via DailyMed; ASHP sources reviewed but contained no administration or monitoring guidance beyond case reports.

reconstitution.label: Preparation Notes
reconstitution.value: Inject diluent slowly; vigorously shake or roll vial until uniform suspension achieved; do not filter after reconstitution as this could remove active substance

reconstitution.label: Immediate Use Stability
reconstitution.value: Room temperature up to 1 hour after reconstitution

reconstitution.label: Refrigerated Stability (Room Temp Diluent)
reconstitution.value: Refrigerate at 2°C to 8°C for up to 8 hours; may equilibrate to room temperature for up to 30 minutes prior to administration

reconstitution.label: Refrigerated Stability (Cold Diluent 2-8°C)
reconstitution.value: Refrigerate at 2°C to 8°C for up to 8 hours; may equilibrate to room temperature for up to 30 minutes prior to administration

dilution.label: Route Options
dilution.value: Subcutaneous injection (reconstituted suspension) or intravenous infusion (requires further dilution of reconstituted solution)

dilution.label: IV Dilution
dilution.value: Product labeled for reconstitution as solution with further dilution for intravenous infusion; specific IV diluent and concentration not detailed in provided text

administration.label: Routes
administration.value: Subcutaneous injection or intravenous infusion

administration.label: SC Administration
administration.value: Rotate injection sites (thigh, abdomen, or upper arm); give new injections at least one inch from old site; avoid tender, bruised, red, or hard areas

administration.label: SC Preparation Before Injection
administration.value: Re-suspend by vigorously rolling syringe between palms immediately prior to administration until uniform cloudy suspension achieved

administration.label: Multiple Vial Doses
administration.value: Divide dose equally between syringes and inject into separate sites (e.g., 150 mg dose = 6 mL in 2 syringes with 3 mL each)

administration.label: Premedication
administration.value: Premedicate for nausea and vomiting

monitoring.labs: Complete blood counts, liver chemistries, and serum creatinine prior to first dose; monitor serum bicarbonate, BUN, and serum creatinine for renal toxicity; monitor hematologic response (WBC, ANC, platelets) and bone marrow biopsy cellularity as indicated

clinicalAlerts: Do not substitute Azacitidine for injection for oral azacitidine; indications and dosing regimens differ
Hazardous drug: follow applicable special handling and disposal procedures
Single-dose vial with no preservatives; discard unused portions properly
Monitor for renal toxicity: if serum bicarbonate <20 mEq/L or unexplained BUN/creatinine elevations occur, reduce dose by 50% or delay next cycle
Elderly patients at higher risk for toxic reactions due to decreased renal function
Dose adjustments required based on hematology values and renal function

citation.primary: Azacitidine for Injection Package Insert. DailyMed, U.S. National Library of Medicine.

azithromycin.sourceNotes: Data extracted from FDA-approved package insert accessed via DailyMed; no ASHP guidelines were available or applicable for this drug.

reconstitution.label: Vial size

dilution.value: 0.9% Sodium Chloride Injection (Normal Saline)

dilution.value: 2 mg/mL

dilution.label: Final concentration for 3-hour infusion

dilution.label: Typical infusion volume
dilution.value: 250 mL or 500 mL

administration.value: Intravenous infusion only

administration.label: Infusion duration (2 mg/mL)
administration.value: 1 hour

administration.label: Concentration-specific infusion requirement
administration.value: 500 mg dose at 2 mg/mL over 1 hour OR at 1 mg/mL over 3 hours

administration.label: Administration caution
administration.value: Do not give as bolus or intramuscular injection

clinicalAlerts: QTc interval prolongation was observed in a dose- and concentration-dependent manner; following 500 mg IV dose given over 1 hour, mean Cmax is higher than after 1,500 mg oral dose, suggesting greater QTc prolongation risk with IV administration
Serum protein binding is variable and concentration-dependent (51% at 0.02 mcg/mL to 7% at 2 mcg/mL)
No dosage adjustment needed for mild to moderate renal impairment; use caution in severe renal impairment (GFR <10 mL/min) where Cmax and AUC are increased 61% and 35% respectively

ivPush.specialConditions: ASHP study demonstrated safety of IVP administration with 1% adverse event rate (n=43 aztreonam administrations); ADE similar to IVPB administration; used during small-volume parenteral solution shortage

monitoring.labs: Serum creatinine, creatinine clearance, BUN, urinalysis, total urinary protein excretion, N-acetyl-β-glucosaminidase, alanine aminopeptidase, β2-microglobulin

sideEffects: Gastrointestinal effects (alterations of anaerobic intestinal flora may decrease colonization resistance, permitting overgrowth of Candida and Clostridium species)
clinicalAlerts: Dosage should be halved in patients with creatinine clearance between 10 and 30 mL/min/1.73 m² after initial loading dose

In severe renal failure (creatinine clearance <10 mL/min/1.73 m²), maintenance dose should be one-fourth of usual initial dose; for serious/life-threatening infections, give one-eighth of initial dose in addition to maintenance doses
Prolonged serum levels may occur in patients with renal insufficiency
Doses smaller than indicated should not be used

basiliximab.drugClass: Immunosuppressive agent; IL-2 receptor antagonist; chimeric monoclonal antibody
basiliximab.sourceNotes: Data extracted from FDA package insert (DailyMed) for basiliximab; ASHP article did not contain clinically relevant administration details.

reconstitution.label: Reconstitution technique
reconstitution.value: Shake vial gently to dissolve powder; solution should be clear-to-opalescent and colorless

dilution.label: Compatible diluents
dilution.value: 0.9% Sodium Chloride Injection, USP or 5% Dextrose Injection, USP

dilution.value: None; must maintain sterility

administration.label: Administration method
administration.value: May be given as bolus injection or intravenous infusion over 20 to 30 minutes

administration.value: Two doses of 20 mg each: first dose within 2 hours prior to transplantation surgery, second dose 4 days after transplantation

administration.label: Pediatric dosing schedule (< 35 kg)
administration.value: Two doses of 10 mg each: first dose within 2 hours prior to transplantation surgery, second dose 4 days after transplantation

administration.label: Pediatric dosing schedule (≥ 35 kg)
administration.value: Two doses of 20 mg each: first dose within 2 hours prior to transplantation surgery, second dose 4 days after transplantation

administration.label: Administration precaution
administration.value: Bolus administration may be associated with nausea, vomiting and local reactions including pain

administration.label: Y-site compatibility
administration.value: Other drug substances should not be added or infused simultaneously through the same intravenous line

ivCompatibility.value: No incompatibility observed

ivCompatibility.label: Infusion sets
ivCompatibility.value: No incompatibility observed

ivCompatibility.value: No data available; do not mix or infuse simultaneously through same line

ivPush.pushRate: May be given as bolus injection (reconstituted solution)
ivPush.dilutionRequired: Reconstitute 10 mg in 2.5 mL or 20 mg in 5 mL Sterile Water for Injection, USP
ivPush.specialConditions: Bolus administration may be associated with nausea, vomiting and local reactions including pain; infusion over 20-30 minutes is alternative method

monitoring.drugLevels: Complete and consistent binding to IL-2Rα maintained when serum levels exceed 0.2 mcg/mL; IL-2Rα sites no longer fully bound when concentrations fall below this threshold
monitoring.vitals: Monitor for hypersensitivity reactions including hypotension and tachycardia
monitoring.duringInfusion: Monitor for severe acute hypersensitivity reactions including anaphylaxis, hypotension, tachycardia, cardiac failure, dyspnea, wheezing, bronchospasm, pulmonary edema, respiratory failure, urticaria, rash, pruritus, and/or sneezing

sideEffects: Nausea and vomiting with bolus administration
Local reactions including pain with bolus administration
blackBoxWarnings: Only physicians experienced in immunosuppression therapy and management of organ transplantation patients should prescribe basiliximab
The physician responsible for basiliximab administration should have complete information requisite for the follow-up of the patient
Patients receiving the drug should be managed in facilities equipped and staffed with adequate laboratory and supportive medical resources
clinicalAlerts: Severe acute hypersensitivity reactions including anaphylaxis have been observed both on initial exposure and following re-exposure after several months
Extreme caution should be exercised in all patients previously given basiliximab when being administered a subsequent course
Patients in whom concomitant immunosuppression was discontinued prematurely may be at particular risk of severe hypersensitivity on re-administration
Medications for treatment of severe hypersensitivity reactions including anaphylaxis should be available for immediate use
If severe hypersensitivity reaction occurs, therapy should be permanently discontinued
Patients on immunosuppressive therapy are at increased risk for lymphoproliferative disorders and opportunistic infections
Second dose should be withheld if complications such as severe hypersensitivity reactions or graft loss occur
Drug does not contain antimicrobial preservatives or bacteriostatic agents; sterility must be maintained

ivCompatibility.value: Compatible for dilution to final concentration 0.05-0.7 mg/mL in 500 mL bag

ivCompatibility.value: No other diluents have been shown to be compatible

monitoring.labs: Complete blood counts (monitor for myelosuppression: lymphopenia, leukopenia, anemia, neutropenia, thrombocytopenia); liver function tests (monitor for hepatotoxicity); electrolytes including potassium (hypokalemia reported in 5% grade 3-4); renal function
monitoring.drugLevels: Therapeutic drug monitoring not routinely performed - bendamustine has intermediate half-life of approximately 40 minutes with active metabolites M3 (t½ ~3 hours) and M4 (t½ ~30 minutes)
monitoring.vitals: Monitor for infusion reactions during and within 24 hours post-infusion; monitor for pyrexia (fever reported in 34% of NHL patients)
monitoring.duringInfusion: Monitor for anaphylaxis and infusion reactions; observe for signs of hypersensitivity including anaphylactic and anaphylactoid reactions

clinicalAlerts: Myelosuppression - delay administration for Grade 4 hematologic toxicity until ANC ≥1 x 10⁹/L and platelets ≥75 x 10⁹/L
Infections - monitor for signs of infection
Progressive Multifocal Leukoencephalopathy - monitor for neurological symptoms

Anaphylaxis and Infusion Reactions - contraindicated in patients with known hypersensitivity to bendamustine or excipients (polyethylene glycol 400, absolute ethanol, sodium hydroxide, monothioglycerol)
Tumor Lysis Syndrome - preventive measures should be taken in at-risk patients per ASHP guidelines
Skin Reactions - monitor for skin toxicity
Hepatotoxicity - monitor liver function
Other Malignancies - secondary malignancies may occur
Extravasation Injury - ensure proper IV access

beractant.sourceNotes: Data extracted from FDA package insert (DailyMed setid 7ef9e3a5-fc39-4ae1-0dad-6b47a1684635); ASHP guidelines reviewed but provided no additional actionable IV administration data as beractant is administered intratracheally only.

reconstitution.label: Reconstitution Required
reconstitution.value: No reconstitution required; supplied as ready-to-use sterile intratracheal suspension

reconstitution.label: Special Handling
reconstitution.value: Store refrigerated 36°F to 46°F (2°C to 8°C); warm to room temperature for at least 20 minutes or in hand for at least 8 minutes before use; do not use artificial warming; swirl gently if settling occurs (DO NOT SHAKE); do not filter; do not remove from refrigerator for more than 24 hours; do not warm and return to refrigerator more than once

dilution.label: Dilution Required
dilution.value: Not applicable - administered undiluted intratracheally

administration.label: Route
administration.value: Intratracheal administration only via 5 French end-hole catheter inserted into endotracheal tube

administration.label: Dose
administration.value: 100 mg phospholipids/kg birth weight (4 mL/kg) per dose; up to 4 doses in first 48 hours, no more frequently than every 6 hours

administration.label: Administration Method
administration.value: Divide dose into four quarter-dose aliquots; administer each aliquot over 2-3 seconds with infant repositioned for each aliquot (head/body inclined 5-10° down then up, head turned right then left); manually ventilate for at least 30 seconds between aliquots until stable

administration.label: Rate Restrictions
administration.value: Each quarter-dose injected gently over 2-3 seconds; allow infant stabilization between aliquots

administration.label: Post-Administration
administration.value: Do not suction for 1 hour after final dose unless signs of significant airway obstruction occur; remove catheter without flushing after final quarter-dose

monitoring.duringInfusion: Frequent monitoring with arterial or transcutaneous measurement of systemic oxygen and carbon dioxide; monitor for transient bradycardia and oxygen desaturation during dosing procedure

clinicalAlerts: Can rapidly affect oxygenation and lung compliance within minutes; use restricted to highly supervised clinical setting with immediate availability of clinicians experienced in intubation, ventilator management, and general care of premature infants
If bradycardia or decreased oxygen saturation occurs during dosing, stop procedure and initiate appropriate measures; resume after stabilization

sideEffects: Major bleeding (intracranial bleeding, retroperitoneal bleeding, clinically overt bleeding with hemoglobin decrease >3 g/dL or transfusion >2 units)
clinicalAlerts: Active major bleeding is a contraindication
Hypersensitivity (e.g., anaphylaxis) to bivalirudin or its components is a contraindication
Coadministration with heparin, warfarin, thrombolytics, or GPIs associated with increased risks of major bleeding
INR measurements made in bivalirudin-treated patients may not be useful for determining appropriate warfarin dose
Approximately 25% bivalirudin is cleared by hemodialysis
Coagulation times return to baseline approximately 1 hour following cessation of bivalirudin administration
