bleomycin.sourceNotes: Data extracted from FDA package insert accessed via DailyMed for bleomycin for injection, USP (setid=640602f9-a7df-46f0-a9b1-19c471008bee).

reconstitution.label: Vial sizes
reconstitution.value: 15 units per vial and 30 units per vial as sterile lyophilized powder

administration.label: Routes
administration.value: Intravenous, intramuscular, subcutaneous, intrapleural

administration.label: Squamous cell carcinoma, non-Hodgkin's lymphoma, testicular carcinoma dosing
administration.value: 0.25 to 0.50 units/kg (10 to 20 units/m²) given IV, IM, or subcutaneously weekly or twice weekly

administration.label: Hodgkin's disease dosing
administration.value: 0.25 to 0.50 units/kg (10 to 20 units/m²) given IV, IM, or subcutaneously weekly or twice weekly; after 50% response, maintenance dose of 1 unit daily or 5 units weekly IV or IM

administration.label: Malignant pleural effusion dosing
administration.value: 60 units administered as a single dose bolus intrapleural injection

administration.label: Test dosing for lymphoma patients
administration.value: 2 units or less for the first 2 doses due to possibility of anaphylactoid reaction; if no acute reaction occurs, then regular dosage schedule may be followed

administration.label: Total dose caution
administration.value: Total doses over 400 units should be given with great caution due to increased pulmonary toxicity; pulmonary toxicities may occur at lower doses when used in combination with other antineoplastic agents

monitoring.labs: Renal function tests and liver function tests should be monitored as renal or hepatic toxicity may occur at any time after initiation of therapy
monitoring.vitals: Blood pressure (hypotension is a component of severe idiosyncratic reaction)
monitoring.duringInfusion: Careful monitoring is essential after first or second dose due to severe idiosyncratic reaction (hypotension, mental confusion, fever, chills, wheezing) occurring in approximately 1% of lymphoma patients; roentgenograms of chest should be taken every 1 to 2 weeks to monitor for pulmonary toxicity; sequential measurement of pulmonary diffusion capacity for carbon monoxide (DLco) is recommended as indicator of subclinical pulmonary toxicity

sideEffects: Pulmonary toxicity (pneumonitis occasionally progressing to pulmonary fibrosis) occurring in approximately 10% of treated patients
Fine rales (earliest sign of pulmonary toxicity)
Decreased total lung volume and vital capacity
blackBoxWarnings: Pulmonary fibrosis is the most severe toxicity associated with bleomycin. The most frequent presentation is pneumonitis occasionally progressing to pulmonary fibrosis. Its occurrence is higher in elderly patients and in those receiving greater than 400 units total dose, but pulmonary toxicity has been observed in young patients and those treated with low doses.
A severe idiosyncratic reaction consisting of hypotension, mental confusion, fever, chills, and wheezing has been reported in approximately 1% of lymphoma patients treated with bleomycin.

It is recommended that Bleomycin for Injection, USP be administered under the supervision of a qualified physician experienced in the use of cancer chemotherapeutic agents. Appropriate management of therapy and complications is possible only when adequate diagnostic and treatment facilities are readily available.
clinicalAlerts: Patients receiving bleomycin must be observed carefully and frequently during and after therapy
Use with extreme caution in patients with significant impairment of renal function or compromised pulmonary function
Approximately 1% of patients treated have died of pulmonary fibrosis
Pulmonary toxicity is both dose and age related, being more common in patients over 70 years of age and in those receiving over 400 units total dose
If pulmonary changes are noted on roentgenogram, treatment should be discontinued until it can be determined if they are drug related
Renal insufficiency markedly alters bleomycin elimination; dosing reductions required for creatinine clearance <50 mL/min
Fatal bleomycin pulmonary toxicity has been reported in a patient with unrecognized cisplatin-induced oliguric renal failure
Pregnancy Category D: bleomycin can cause fetal harm when administered to a pregnant woman

citation.primary: Bleomycin for Injection, USP [package insert]. FDA via DailyMed.

reconstitution.label: Formulation
reconstitution.value: Supplied as sterile solution 0.25 mg/mL in 4 mL and 10 mL vials — no reconstitution required

administration.label: IV Administration Rate
administration.value: Intravenous administration should be given over a period of 1 to 2 minutes

administration.label: Usual Initial Dose
administration.value: 0.5 mg to 1 mg IV or IM

administration.label: Repeat Dosing
administration.value: Second or third dose may be given at intervals of 2 to 3 hours if response is insufficient

administration.value: Should not exceed 10 mg daily

administration.value: Diuresis starts within minutes following IV injection and reaches maximum levels within 15 to 30 minutes

ivCompatibility.label: 5% Dextrose in Water
ivCompatibility.value: Compatible in glass and PVC containers — use within 24 hours

ivCompatibility.label: 0.9% Sodium Chloride
ivCompatibility.value: Compatible in glass and PVC containers — use within 24 hours

ivCompatibility.label: Lactated Ringer's Solution
ivCompatibility.value: Compatible in glass and PVC containers — use within 24 hours

ivCompatibility.label: Isavuconazonium Sulfate (Y-site in 5% Dextrose)
ivCompatibility.value: Incompatible with isavuconazonium sulfate during simulated Y-site administration in 5% dextrose injection

ivPush.specialConditions: Excessive doses or too frequent administration can lead to profound water loss, electrolyte depletion, dehydration, reduction in blood volume and circulatory collapse

monitoring.labs: Serum potassium should be measured periodically. Periodic determinations of other electrolytes are advised in patients treated with high doses or for prolonged periods, particularly in those on low-salt diets. Reversible elevations of BUN and creatinine may occur. May increase urinary calcium excretion with resultant hypocalcemia. May increase urinary excretion of magnesium resulting in hypomagnesemia.

sideEffects: Encephalopathy in patients with preexisting liver disease (0.6%)
blackBoxWarnings: Bumetanide Injection, USP is a potent diuretic which, if given in excessive amounts, can lead to a profound diuresis with water and electrolyte depletion. Therefore, careful medical supervision is required, and dose and dosage schedule have to be adjusted to the individual patient's needs.
clinicalAlerts: Excessive doses or too frequent administration can lead to profound water loss, electrolyte depletion, dehydration, reduction in blood volume and circulatory collapse with the possibility of vascular thrombosis and embolism, particularly in elderly patients

Hypokalemia can occur — prevention requires particular attention in patients receiving digitalis and diuretics for CHF, hepatic cirrhosis and ascites, states of aldosterone excess, potassium-losing nephropathy, and certain diarrheal states

In patients with hepatic cirrhosis and ascites, sudden alterations of electrolyte balance may precipitate hepatic encephalopathy and coma — treatment best initiated in hospital with small doses and careful monitoring
Ototoxicity potential exists especially at high doses repeated frequently in the face of renal excretory function impairment — bumetanide has been shown to produce ototoxicity in animals
Patients allergic to sulfonamides may show hypersensitivity to bumetanide
Contraindicated in anuria, hepatic coma, severe electrolyte depletion, and hypersensitivity to the drug

citation.primary: Bumetanide Injection, USP Package Insert. DailyMed, National Library of Medicine.
citation.ashp: So W, Kim L, Thabit AK, Nicolau DP, Kuti JL. Physical compatibility of isavuconazonium sulfate with select i.v. drugs during simulated Y-site administration. Am J Health Syst Pharm. 2017;74(1):e55-e63.

administration.label: Route
administration.value: Intravenous (IV) or Intramuscular (IM)

administration.label: IV infusion rate
administration.value: Slow intravenous injection over at least 2 minutes

administration.value: Deep intramuscular injection

administration.value: 0.3 mg (1 mL) given IV or IM at up to 6-hour intervals as needed

administration.value: Up to 0.6 mg (intramuscular only, non-high-risk patients)

administration.label: Pediatric dosing (2-12 years)
administration.value: 2 to 6 mcg/kg every 4 to 6 hours

administration.label: High-risk patient caution
administration.value: In elderly, debilitated, respiratory disease, or immediate postoperative patients, limit dose to minimum required; extra caution with IV route

ivPush.pushRate: Slow intravenous injection over at least 2 minutes
ivPush.specialConditions: Extra caution with IV route, particularly with initial dose. High-risk patients (elderly, debilitated, respiratory disease, immediate postoperative) should receive minimum required dose.

monitoring.vitals: Monitor blood pressure and pulse rate (may cause decrease or rarely increase in pulse rate and blood pressure); monitor for orthostatic hypotension
monitoring.duringInfusion: Monitor for respiratory depression, especially with initial dose and dose increases

sideEffects: Reduction in motility of gastrointestinal tract
clinicalAlerts: Schedule III controlled substance with risk of addiction, abuse, and misuse
Respiratory depression can occur at any time during therapy, especially when initiating and following dosage increases
Use lowest effective dosage for shortest duration consistent with treatment goals
Assess each patient's risk for opioid addiction, abuse, or misuse prior to prescribing
Very slow rate of dissociation from receptor accounts for longer duration of action than morphine and unpredictability of reversal by opioid antagonists
May cause QT prolongation ≤15 msec
May cause androgen deficiency with extended use
Anesthetic agents (e.g., 0.5% halothane) decrease hepatic blood flow by about 30%, affecting buprenorphine clearance

administration.label: Administration
administration.value: Should be administered by a healthcare provider

administration.label: Maximum Volume Per Injection
administration.value: 1.5 mL

administration.label: Multiple Injections
administration.value: If multiple injections are required, administer at different injection sites

monitoring.labs: Fasting serum phosphorus: Pediatric XLH - every 4 weeks for first 3 months, then as appropriate. Adult XLH - monthly (measured 2 weeks post-dose) for first 3 months, then as appropriate. TIO patients - monthly (measured 2 weeks post-dose) for first 3 months, then as appropriate. In patients at high risk for hypercalcemia, assess serum calcium and parathyroid hormone levels prior to starting.

clinicalAlerts: Prior to starting CRYSVITA, discontinue oral phosphate and/or active vitamin D analogs for one week
Prior to starting CRYSVITA, fasting serum phosphorus concentration should be below the reference range for age
Contraindicated in patients with severe renal impairment or end stage renal disease
Risk of hypercalcemia in patients with pre-existing hyperparathyroidism, prolonged immobilization, dehydration, hypervitaminosis D, or renal impairment

busulfan.sourceNotes: Data extracted from FDA package insert available on DailyMed for busulfan injection; ASHP sources reviewed but contained no applicable IV push or monitoring guidance.

dilution.label: Compatible Diluents
dilution.value: 0.9% Sodium Chloride Injection, USP (normal saline) or 5% Dextrose Injection, USP (D5W)

dilution.label: Dilution Volume
dilution.value: Diluent quantity should be 10 times the volume of busulfan injection

dilution.label: Final Concentration
dilution.value: Approximately 0.5 mg/mL

dilution.label: pH Range (diluted)
dilution.value: 3.4 to 5.5 (reflects pH of diluent used)

dilution.label: Preparation Instructions
dilution.value: Always add busulfan injection to the diluent, not the diluent to busulfan injection. Mix thoroughly by inverting several times.

dilution.label: Incompatibility Warning
dilution.value: Incompatible with polycarbonate. Do not use any infusion components (syringes, filter needles, IV tubing, etc.) containing polycarbonate.

dilution.label: Administration Set
dilution.value: Use an administration set with minimal residual hold-up volume (2 mL to 5 mL)

dilution.label: Storage After Dilution
dilution.value: Discard unused portion

administration.label: Route
administration.value: Intravenous infusion via a central venous catheter

administration.label: Infusion Duration
administration.value: 2 hours

administration.label: Infusion Rate Control
administration.value: Infusion pumps should be used to administer the diluted busulfan injection solution

administration.label: Catheter Flushing
administration.value: Prior to and following each infusion, flush the indwelling catheter line with approximately 5 mL of 0.9% Sodium Chloride Injection, USP or 5% Dextrose Injection, USP

administration.label: Concomitant Infusion Restriction
administration.value: DO NOT infuse concomitantly with another intravenous solution of unknown compatibility

administration.label: Rate Restriction
administration.value: RAPID INFUSION OF BUSULFAN INJECTION HAS NOT BEEN TESTED AND IS NOT RECOMMENDED

administration.label: Push/Bolus Restriction
administration.value: Do not administer as intravenous push or bolus

ivCompatibility.label: Polycarbonate
ivCompatibility.value: Incompatible with polycarbonate components (syringes, filter needles, IV tubing, etc.)

ivCompatibility.label: 0.9% Sodium Chloride Injection, USP
ivCompatibility.value: Compatible for dilution and catheter flushing

ivCompatibility.label: 5% Dextrose Injection, USP
ivCompatibility.value: Compatible for dilution and catheter flushing

ivCompatibility.label: Other IV Solutions
ivCompatibility.value: Do not infuse concomitantly with another intravenous solution of unknown compatibility

monitoring.drugLevels: AUC monitoring recommended; 93% of patients maintained AUC values below target of 1500 μM•min at dose 9 to minimize risk of hepatic veno-occlusive disease (HVOD)

blackBoxWarnings: MYELOSUPPRESSION: Busulfan injection causes severe and prolonged myelosuppression at the recommended dosage. Hematopoietic progenitor cell transplantation is required to prevent potentially fatal complications of the prolonged myelosuppression.
clinicalAlerts: Seizures: Premedicate patients with anticonvulsants (e.g., benzodiazepines, phenytoin, valproic acid or levetiracetam) 12 hours prior to busulfan injection to 24 hours after the last dose
Hepatic Veno-Occlusive Disease (HVOD): Monitor for signs and symptoms; maintaining AUC less than 1500 μM•min minimizes risk
Cardiac Tamponade: Monitor for cardiovascular complications
Bronchopulmonary Dysplasia: Monitor for pulmonary complications
Cellular Dysplasia: Monitor for cellular changes
Skin reactions may occur with accidental exposure. Use gloves when preparing. If busulfan or diluted solution contacts skin or mucosa, wash thoroughly with water
Administer antiemetics prior to first dose and continue on fixed schedule through administration

citation.primary: Busulfan Injection [package insert]. Eugia US LLC.

cabazitaxel.drugClass: antineoplastic agent, taxane class, microtubule inhibitor

dilution.label: Equipment Restriction
dilution.value: PVC equipment should not be used

administration.label: Route

administration.label: Dosing Schedule

monitoring.duringInfusion: Monitor for hypersensitivity reactions during infusion

clinicalAlerts: Premedicate at least 30 minutes prior to each dose with antihistamine (dexchlorpheniramine 5 mg or diphenhydramine 25 mg or equivalent), corticosteroid (dexamethasone 8 mg or equivalent), and H2 antagonist
Primary prophylaxis with G-CSF recommended in patients with high-risk clinical features; consider in all patients receiving 25 mg/m² dose
Contraindicated in severe hepatic impairment (total bilirubin >3 × ULN)
Avoid coadministration with strong CYP3A inhibitors; if required, consider 25% dose reduction
PVC equipment should not be used for preparation or administration
Hazardous drug: follow special handling and disposal procedures

administration.label: Loading dose route and rate

administration.label: Maintenance dose
administration.value: 0.25 mL/kg (5 mg/kg caffeine citrate = 2.5 mg/kg caffeine base) every 24 hours, beginning 24 hours after loading dose

ivCompatibility.value: Chemically stable for 24 hours at room temperature

monitoring.labs: Baseline serum caffeine levels in infants previously treated with theophylline or born to mothers who consumed caffeine prior to delivery
monitoring.drugLevels: Serum caffeine concentrations monitored periodically throughout treatment to avoid toxicity; serious toxicity associated with levels >50 mg/L
monitoring.duringInfusion: Monitor for development of necrotizing enterocolitis

clinicalAlerts: Necrotizing enterocolitis: During clinical trial, 6 cases developed (3 deaths); 5 of 6 patients were exposed to caffeine citrate. Causal relationship not established, but preterm infants should be carefully monitored.
Use with caution in preterm neonates with impaired renal or hepatic function; adjust dosing and monitor serum concentrations to avoid toxicity.

Measure baseline serum caffeine levels in infants previously treated with theophylline (preterm infants metabolize theophylline to caffeine) or born to mothers who consumed caffeine (caffeine crosses placenta).

calcitonin.sourceNotes: Data extracted from DailyMed FDA package insert (setid=1b8c8c83-1a46-4c0a-b883-e7dce781c5d1).

administration.label: Route
administration.value: Intramuscular or subcutaneous injection (also available as nasal spray)

administration.label: Hypercalcemia
administration.value: 4 IU/kg IM or SC every 12 hours; may increase to 8 IU/kg every 12 hours if needed

administration.label: Skin test
administration.value: Consider skin test before first dose (1 IU intradermally)

administration.label: Note
administration.value: Tachyphylaxis common after 48 hours — effect diminishes

monitoring.labs: Monitor serum calcium; monitor for hypocalcemia

clinicalAlerts: Tachyphylaxis occurs within 48-72 hours — calcitonin is a bridge therapy, not definitive treatment for hypercalcemia
Consider skin test before initial dose — allergic reactions reported
IM or SC only — NOT for IV use
Primarily a temporary measure until bisphosphonate takes effect

calcium chloride.sourceNotes: Data extracted from Ringer's Injection USP package insert containing calcium chloride as an active ingredient; no ASHP-specific calcium chloride IV push or monitoring guidelines were provided in source documents.

dilution.label: Compatible Diluent
dilution.value: Calcium chloride is supplied as a component of Ringer's Injection USP, a ready-to-use sterile solution containing 0.33 g/L calcium chloride dihydrate

dilution.label: Final Concentration in Ringer's Injection
dilution.value: 4.5 mEq/L calcium (from 0.33 g/L calcium chloride dihydrate)

dilution.label: Solution Osmolarity
dilution.value: 310 mOsmol/L (calculated)

administration.label: Route
administration.value: Intravenous use only

administration.label: Administration Instructions
administration.value: Use immediately after opening the container. Discard unused portion. Replace IV administration apparatus at least once every 24 hours.

administration.label: Infusion Rate
administration.value: Based on patient's age, weight, clinical condition, and calculated maintenance or replacement fluid requirements

administration.label: Air Embolism Prevention
administration.value: Use non-vented infusion set or close vent on vented set. Use dedicated line without connections. If pressure infusion required, evacuate all air from bag before initiation. Turn off pump before container is empty.

ivCompatibility.label: Ceftriaxone in neonates ≤28 days
ivCompatibility.value: Do not administer simultaneously due to risk of fatal ceftriaxone-calcium salt precipitation in bloodstream

ivCompatibility.label: Ceftriaxone in patients >28 days
ivCompatibility.value: May be administered sequentially if infusion lines are thoroughly flushed between infusions with compatible fluid

ivCompatibility.label: Citrate anticoagulated/preserved blood
ivCompatibility.value: Do not administer simultaneously through same administration set due to likelihood of coagulation precipitated by calcium content

monitoring.labs: Serum potassium, serum sodium, serum calcium, electrolyte concentrations especially during prolonged use
monitoring.vitals: Clinical status, fluid balance

clinicalAlerts: Contraindicated in neonates ≤28 days receiving concomitant ceftriaxone due to risk of fatal ceftriaxone-calcium salt precipitation
Risk of air embolism - follow strict preparation and administration instructions
Hypersensitivity reactions possible
Risk of hyperkalemia, especially with potassium-sparing diuretics, ACE inhibitors, ARBs, or calcineurin inhibitors
Risk of hyponatremia with concomitant diuretics or SIADH-inducing drugs
Risk of hypercalcemia with thiazide diuretics or vitamin D
Increased digoxin toxicity risk - may require reduced volume/rate of administration
Avoid in lithium-treated patients - may decrease lithium levels

calcium gluconate.brandName: Calcium Gluconate in Sodium Chloride Injection
calcium gluconate.sourceNotes: Data extracted from FDA package insert (DailyMed) for Calcium Gluconate in Sodium Chloride Injection. ASHP guidelines reviewed but did not contain relevant IV push or monitoring guidance for calcium gluconate.

reconstitution.label: Reconstitution Required
reconstitution.value: No reconstitution required — supplied as ready-to-use solution

dilution.label: Dilution Required
dilution.value: Do not dilute Calcium Gluconate in Sodium Chloride Injection prior to use. Any unused portion should be discarded.

dilution.label: Supplied Concentrations
dilution.value: 1,000 mg per 50 mL (20 mg/mL calcium gluconate; 1.86 mg/mL elemental calcium) OR 2,000 mg per 100 mL (20 mg/mL calcium gluconate; 1.86 mg/mL elemental calcium)

administration.label: Route
administration.value: Intravenous (bolus or continuous infusion) via a secure intravenous line

administration.label: Bolus Infusion Rate — Adults
administration.value: DO NOT exceed 200 mg/minute

administration.label: Bolus Infusion Rate — Pediatric
administration.value: DO NOT exceed 100 mg/minute

administration.label: Continuous Infusion Rate — Adults
administration.value: Initiate at 5.4 mg/kg/hour to 21.5 mg/kg/hour; adjust rate as needed based on serum calcium levels

administration.label: Continuous Infusion Rate — Pediatric (>1 month to <17 years)
administration.value: Initiate at 8 mg/kg/hour to 13 mg/kg/hour; adjust rate as needed based on serum calcium levels

administration.label: Continuous Infusion Rate — Neonates (<1 month to 1 month)
administration.value: Initiate at 17 mg/kg/hour to 33 mg/kg/hour; adjust rate as needed based on serum calcium levels

administration.label: Administration Precautions
administration.value: Administer via a secure intravenous line to avoid calcinosis cutis and tissue necrosis. Monitor patients, vitals, calcium, and ECG during infusion.

ivCompatibility.label: Ceftriaxone
ivCompatibility.value: Do not mix with ceftriaxone. Concurrent use can lead to ceftriaxone-calcium precipitates. Contraindicated in neonates (≤28 days). In patients >28 days, may be administered sequentially with thorough line flushing; must not be administered simultaneously via Y-site.

ivCompatibility.label: Bicarbonate-containing fluids
ivCompatibility.value: Do not mix with fluids containing bicarbonate. Precipitation may result.

ivCompatibility.label: Phosphate-containing fluids
ivCompatibility.value: Do not mix with fluids containing phosphate. Precipitation may result.

ivCompatibility.label: Minocycline injection
ivCompatibility.value: Do not mix with minocycline injection. Calcium complexes minocycline rendering it inactive.

monitoring.labs: Serum calcium every 4 to 6 hours during intermittent infusions; serum calcium every 1 to 4 hours during continuous infusion. For patients with renal impairment, monitor serum calcium levels every 4 hours.
monitoring.vitals: Monitor patients, vitals, calcium, and ECG during infusion
monitoring.duringInfusion: Monitor patients, vitals, calcium, and ECG during infusion. Monitor ECG closely during administration if given in patients receiving cardiac glycosides.

clinicalAlerts: Hypercalcemia increases the risk of digoxin toxicity. Synergistic arrhythmias may occur if calcium and cardiac glycosides are administered together. Avoid administration in patients receiving cardiac glycosides; if necessary, administer slowly in small amounts and monitor ECG closely.
Concurrent use of ceftriaxone and IV calcium-containing products is contraindicated in neonates (≤28 days) due to risk of ceftriaxone-calcium precipitates causing end-organ damage.
Administer via secure IV line to avoid calcinosis cutis and tissue necrosis from extravasation.
Hypotension, bradycardia, and cardiac arrhythmias may occur. Monitor vitals and ECG during infusion.
Risk of aluminum toxicity in patients with renal impairment.
Calcium channel blockers: administration of calcium may reduce response to calcium channel blockers.
Drugs that may cause hypercalcemia (vitamin D, vitamin A, thiazide diuretics, estrogen, calcipotriene, teriparatide): monitor plasma calcium concentrations.

citation.primary: Calcium Gluconate in Sodium Chloride Injection [package insert]. WG Critical Care, LLC.

calfactant.sourceNotes: Data extracted from FDA package insert (DailyMed) for calfactant intratracheal suspension; ASHP guidelines provided pharmacology context but no additional administration details beyond label.

reconstitution.label: Preparation
reconstitution.value: Gently swirl or agitate vial for redispersion. Do not shake. Do not dilute or sonicate.

reconstitution.label: Withdrawal Technique
reconstitution.value: Use 20-gauge or larger needle and syringe to avoid excessive foaming

reconstitution.label: Appearance
reconstitution.value: Off-white suspension; visible flecks and foaming at surface are normal

reconstitution.label: Temperature
reconstitution.value: Does not need to reach room temperature before administration

dilution.label: Dilution
dilution.value: Do not dilute

administration.label: Route
administration.value: Intratracheal only through endotracheal tube

administration.label: Dose
administration.value: 3 mL/kg body weight at birth

administration.label: Frequency
administration.value: Every 12 hours for up to 3 doses total

administration.label: Timing
administration.value: Within 30 minutes after birth for preterm neonates <29 weeks gestational age at risk for RDS

administration.label: Method 1 - Side-Port Adapter
administration.value: Two equal aliquots of 1.5 mL/kg each through side-port adapter. Position neonate with right or left side dependent during and after each aliquot. Maintain ventilation over 20-30 breaths for each aliquot with small bursts timed during inspiratory cycles. Reposition to other side between aliquots.

administration.label: Method 2 - Feeding Catheter
administration.value: Four equal aliquots of 0.75 mL/kg each through 5-French feeding catheter inserted into endotracheal tube. Position neonate in four different positions (prone, supine, right lateral, left lateral) during and after each aliquot. Remove catheter between instillations and resume mechanical ventilation for 0.5-2 minutes.

administration.label: Personnel Requirements
administration.value: Should be administered by healthcare providers experienced in acute care of neonates with RDS requiring intubation. Two attendants present: one to instill, one to monitor.

administration.label: Post-Administration Monitoring
administration.value: Frequently monitor neonate oxygenation and ventilatory status

monitoring.vitals: Frequently monitor neonate oxygenation and ventilatory status after administration
monitoring.duringInfusion: Monitor for cyanosis, airway obstruction, bradycardia, reflux of surfactant into endotracheal tube, requirement for manual ventilation

sideEffects: Reflux of surfactant into endotracheal tube (21%)
clinicalAlerts: Acute changes in oxygenation and lung compliance may occur during administration
Intraventricular hemorrhage and periventricular leukomalacia are known complications
Discard unopened vials stored at room temperature for more than 24 hours
Discard unused product after initial vial entry
Do not remove from refrigerator for more than 24 hours
Do not re-refrigerate more than once

ivCompatibility.label: Heparin (unfractionated)

ivCompatibility.label: Bivalirudin
ivCompatibility.value: Co-administered without clinically detectable interactions

ivCompatibility.value: Co-administered with no evidence of effect on PK/PD of cangrelor

ivCompatibility.label: Nitroglycerin
ivCompatibility.value: Co-administered with no evidence of effect on PK/PD of cangrelor

monitoring.labs: Bleeding parameters
monitoring.duringInfusion: Monitor for signs of active bleeding

clinicalAlerts: Contraindicated in patients with significant active bleeding
Contraindicated in patients with known hypersensitivity to cangrelor or any component
Greater incidence of bleeding with cangrelor than with clopidogrel
Clopidogrel or prasugrel loading doses should be given immediately after discontinuation of cangrelor infusion to avoid gap in platelet inhibition
Ticagrelor can be administered at any time during cangrelor infusion or immediately after discontinuation
Anti-platelet effect decreases rapidly after infusion discontinuation with platelet function returning to normal within 1 hour
Administer via dedicated IV line only

capmatinib.sourceNotes: Data extracted from FDA package insert (DailyMed) for capmatinib (TABRECTA). No IV formulation or administration information is available in the label as this is an oral-only product.

monitoring.labs: ALT, AST, total bilirubin; lipase and amylase

clinicalAlerts: ILD/Pneumonitis: Permanently discontinue TABRECTA for any grade ILD/pneumonitis
Hepatotoxicity: Monitor ALT, AST, and bilirubin; withhold or permanently discontinue based on severity and recovery
Pancreatic Toxicity: Monitor lipase and amylase; withhold or permanently discontinue for Grade 3 or 4 elevations or pancreatitis
Hypersensitivity reactions: Withhold for suspected hypersensitivity; permanently discontinue for serious hypersensitivity reactions
Fatal adverse reactions occurred in 0.5% of patients, including pneumonitis (0.3%)

captopril.sourceNotes: Data extracted from FDA package insert for captopril and hydrochlorothiazide combination tablets; no IV formulation exists for captopril.
clinicalAlerts: Captopril is an oral medication only; no IV formulation is available.
Angioedema involving tongue, glottis, or larynx may be fatal; emergency epinephrine may be required.
Neutropenia/agranulocytosis risk increases with renal impairment and collagen vascular disease.
Anaphylactoid reactions reported during desensitization and membrane exposure (dialysis with high-flux membranes).
Should be taken one hour before meals; food reduces absorption by 30-40%.

carbamazepine.drugClass: Anticonvulsant and analgesic for trigeminal neuralgia
carbamazepine.sourceNotes: Data extracted from FDA package insert for carbamazepine tablets; ASHP reference addressed enteral feeding tubes only and did not contain IV administration data.

monitoring.labs: Complete blood count with monitoring for aplastic anemia and agranulocytosis; HLA-B*1502 genetic screening prior to treatment in patients with ancestry in genetically at-risk Asian populations
monitoring.drugLevels: Therapeutic plasma levels 4-12 mcg/mL; monitoring recommended to increase efficacy and safety

blackBoxWarnings: Serious and sometimes fatal dermatologic reactions including toxic epidermal necrolysis (TEN) and Stevens-Johnson syndrome (SJS) have been reported. Risk estimated at 1-6 per 10,000 new users in Caucasian populations, approximately 10 times higher in some Asian countries. Strong association with HLA-B*1502 allele in patients of Chinese ancestry. Patients with ancestry in genetically at-risk populations should be screened for HLA-B*1502 prior to treatment.

Aplastic anemia and agranulocytosis have been reported. Risk of developing these reactions is 5-8 times greater than in general population. Overall population risk is approximately 6 patients per one million per year for agranulocytosis and 2 patients per one million per year for aplastic anemia.
clinicalAlerts: Carbamazepine is available for oral administration only as tablets and suspension
No intravenous formulation exists for carbamazepine
Carbamazepine suspension should not be administered simultaneously with other liquid medications or diluents; precipitate formation reported with liquid chlorpromazine or thioridazine

carboplatin.sourceNotes: Data extracted from FDA package insert accessed via DailyMed (setid=1c275825-0275-4c73-b650-e4c1519f13b8).

reconstitution.label: Supplied form
reconstitution.value: Ready-to-use sterile solution, 10 mg/mL, no reconstitution required

reconstitution.label: Vial sizes
reconstitution.value: 50 mg/5 mL, 150 mg/15 mL, 450 mg/45 mL, or 600 mg/60 mL multiuse vials

dilution.label: Compatible diluents
dilution.value: Not specified in package insert

dilution.label: Final concentration range
dilution.value: Not specified in package insert

dilution.label: Stability
dilution.value: Not specified in package insert

administration.label: Route
administration.value: Intravenous infusion

administration.label: Infusion duration
administration.value: 15 minutes or longer

administration.label: Hydration requirements
administration.value: No pre- or post-treatment hydration or forced diuresis is required

administration.label: Aluminum restriction
administration.value: Aluminum reacts with carboplatin causing precipitate formation and loss of potency; needles or IV sets containing aluminum parts that may contact the drug must not be used

ivCompatibility.label: Aluminum-containing devices
ivCompatibility.value: Causes precipitate formation and loss of potency

monitoring.labs: Peripheral blood counts (leukocyte, neutrophil, and platelet counts) should be frequently monitored during treatment and until recovery is achieved. Median nadir occurs at day 21. Renal function (creatinine clearance/GFR) should be assessed for dosing adjustments. Liver function tests should be monitored as high doses can cause severe abnormalities.
monitoring.vitals: Monitor for allergic reactions including anaphylaxis, particularly in patients previously exposed to platinum therapy; reactions may occur within minutes of administration
monitoring.duringInfusion: Monitor for allergic/anaphylactic reactions during administration; epinephrine, corticosteroids, and antihistamines should be available

sideEffects: Bone marrow suppression (leukopenia, neutropenia, thrombocytopenia) - dose-dependent and dose-limiting
Anemia - cumulative, may require transfusion support
Emesis/vomiting - frequent, severity increased in patients with prior emetogenic therapy
Peripheral neurotoxicity - infrequent, increased incidence in patients >65 years or previously treated with cisplatin
Renal toxicity - limited nephrotoxic potential (increased with concomitant aminoglycosides)
Audiologic toxicity/hearing loss - reported in pediatric patients at higher than recommended doses with ototoxic agents
Vision loss - reported with doses higher than recommended, usually recovers within weeks
Allergic reactions including anaphylaxis - may occur within minutes of administration
blackBoxWarnings: Should be administered under supervision of qualified physician experienced in cancer chemotherapeutic agents; adequate treatment facilities must be readily available
Bone marrow suppression is dose-related and may be severe, resulting in infection and/or bleeding
Anemia may be cumulative and may require transfusion support
Vomiting is frequent drug-related side effect
Anaphylactic-like reactions may occur within minutes of administration; epinephrine, corticosteroids, and antihistamines should be available
clinicalAlerts: Single intermittent courses should not be repeated until neutrophil count ≥2,000 and platelet count ≥100,000
Initial dosages should be reduced in patients with prior therapy (especially cisplatin-containing regimens), impaired kidney function (creatinine clearance <60 mL/min), or elderly patients
Concomitant use with aminoglycosides increases renal and/or audiologic toxicity
Increased risk of allergic reactions in patients previously exposed to platinum therapy
May cause fetal harm; women of childbearing potential should avoid pregnancy
Use aluminum-free administration sets and needles only

citation.primary: Carboplatin injection [package insert]. DailyMed, National Library of Medicine.

carfilzomib.sourceNotes: Data extracted from FDA package insert (DailyMed) for reconstitution, dilution, administration, monitoring, and adverse effects; ASHP article (Am J Health Syst Pharm 2015) supplemented IV push information and peripheral neuropathy clinical notes.

reconstitution.label: Appearance after reconstitution

administration.value: Not more than 1.66 mg/m²/min

administration.value: Administration over less than 2 hours can lead to pain and burning at injection site

administration.value: Monitor the injected area during administration

monitoring.labs: Blood counts weekly for at least 6 weeks after each dose; renal function prior to administration and periodically during treatment; transaminases and bilirubin periodically during treatment
monitoring.duringInfusion: Monitor the injected area during administration

sideEffects: Pain and burning at injection site (if administered too rapidly)
blackBoxWarnings: Myelosuppression: Carmustine causes suppression of marrow function (including thrombocytopenia and leukopenia), which may contribute to bleeding and overwhelming infections. Monitor blood counts weekly for at least 6 weeks after each dose. Do not administer repeat course until blood counts recover.

Pulmonary Toxicity: Carmustine causes dose-related pulmonary toxicity. Patients receiving greater than 1400 mg/m² cumulative dose are at significantly higher risk. Delayed pulmonary toxicity can occur years after treatment and can result in death, particularly in patients treated in childhood.
clinicalAlerts: Hematologic toxicity can be delayed and cumulative
Do not administer repeat course until platelets above 100 Gi/L, leukocytes above 4 Gi/L, and absolute neutrophil count above 1 Gi/L
Discontinue if creatinine clearance is less than 10 mL/min
Accidental skin contact causes transient hyperpigmentation; wear impervious gloves when handling
Premedicate each dose with anti-emetics
Cytotoxic drug - follow special handling and disposal procedures

monitoring.labs: Monitor liver function tests (ALT, AST, alkaline phosphatase, total bilirubin); monitor for hepatic effects especially in patients with pre-existing hepatic impairment or when co-administered with cyclosporine
monitoring.duringInfusion: Monitor patients for histamine-mediated symptoms including rash, facial swelling, pruritus, sensation of warmth, or bronchospasm during infusion

sideEffects: Histamine-mediated symptoms (rash, facial swelling, pruritus, sensation of warmth, bronchospasm)
Elevated liver enzymes (ALT, AST, alkaline phosphatase)
clinicalAlerts: Hepatic Effects: Cases of clinically significant hepatic dysfunction, hepatitis, and hepatic failure have been reported; monitor liver function tests
Cyclosporine co-administration: Transient increases in ALT and AST observed; use together only if benefit outweighs risk and monitor liver enzymes closely
Elevated histamine levels: Monitor for histamine-mediated reactions during infusion
Hypersensitivity reactions: Anaphylaxis including shock has been reported; discontinue if reaction occurs
Dosage adjustment required for moderate hepatic impairment (Child-Pugh score 7-9): Reduce daily dose to 35 mg after standard loading dose

Drug interactions with enzyme inducers: Rifampin, efavirenz, nevirapine, phenytoin, dexamethasone, and carbamazepine may reduce caspofungin concentrations; consider increasing caspofungin dose to 70 mg daily when co-administered in adults
Tacrolimus interaction: Caspofungin reduces tacrolimus blood concentrations; monitor tacrolimus levels and adjust dose accordingly

ivCompatibility.value: Incompatible with cefotetan disodium per ASHP reference (Am J Health Syst Pharm. 1995 Jun 15;52(12):1347).

ivCompatibility.value: During infusion of cefotetan, it is advisable to discontinue temporarily the administration of other solutions at the same site.

clinicalAlerts: BEFORE THERAPY WITH CEFOTETAN IS INSTITUTED, CAREFUL INQUIRY SHOULD BE MADE TO DETERMINE WHETHER THE PATIENT HAS HAD PREVIOUS HYPERSENSITIVITY REACTIONS TO CEFOTETAN, CEPHALOSPORINS, PENICILLINS, OR OTHER DRUGS.

IF THIS PRODUCT IS TO BE GIVEN TO PENICILLIN-SENSITIVE PATIENTS, CAUTION SHOULD BE EXERCISED BECAUSE CROSS-HYPERSENSITIVITY AMONG BETA-LACTAM ANTIBIOTICS HAS BEEN CLEARLY DOCUMENTED AND MAY OCCUR IN UP TO 10% OF PATIENTS WITH A HISTORY OF PENICILLIN ALLERGY.
IF AN ALLERGIC REACTION TO CEFOTETAN OCCURS, DISCONTINUE THE DRUG.
Most strains of C. difficile are resistant to cefotetan.
Solutions of cefotetan must not be admixed with solutions containing aminoglycosides.

dilution.value: Constitute the 1 g or 2 g vial with Sterile Water for Injection, then add appropriate quantity to IV container with compatible IV fluid

dilution.label: Compatible IV Fluids
dilution.value: Refer to COMPATIBILITY AND STABILITY section of package insert (specific fluids listed in unexcerpted portion)

administration.label: IV Direct Intermittent (Push)
administration.value: Slowly inject directly into vein over 3 to 5 minutes

administration.label: IV Direct via Y-Site
administration.value: May give through tubing of administration set while patient is receiving compatible IV fluid

administration.label: IV Intermittent Infusion
administration.value: Administer as IV infusion over 20 to 30 minutes

administration.label: IM Administration
administration.value: Deep IM injection into large muscle mass (upper outer quadrant of gluteus maximus or lateral thigh)

administration.label: Route Restriction
administration.value: Intra-arterial administration should be avoided

ivCompatibility.label: Y-Site Administration
ivCompatibility.value: Compatible with certain IV fluids during intermittent infusion with Y-type administration set

ivCompatibility.label: Chloramphenicol
ivCompatibility.value: Antagonistic effects observed in vitro with combination of chloramphenicol and ceftazidime

ivPush.dilutionRequired: Constitute with Sterile Water for Injection per Table 5

clinicalAlerts: Intra-arterial administration should be avoided
In vitro antagonistic effects observed with chloramphenicol combination

citation.primary: Ceftazidime for Injection, USP Package Insert
citation.ashp: Bookstaver PB, et al. Am J Health Syst Pharm. 2013;70(24):2185-98 — antimicrobial lock stability only, no IV push or monitoring data

ceftazidime avibactam.sourceNotes: Data extracted from DailyMed FDA package insert (setid=d9c2803f-dc9c-4b19-b4a3-8303bc8c15fd).

reconstitution.label: Reconstitution
reconstitution.value: Reconstitute with 10 mL SWFI, NS, D5W, LR, or D5/0.45%NS

reconstitution.label: Shake to dissolve
reconstitution.value: Shake to dissolve; solution may require up to 2 minutes

dilution.label: Further dilution
dilution.value: Transfer to IV bag of NS, D5W, LR, or D5/0.45%NS (total volume 50-250 mL)

dilution.label: Final concentration
dilution.value: 8-40 mg/mL of ceftazidime component

administration.label: Route
administration.value: Intravenous infusion

administration.label: Adult dose
administration.value: 2.5 g (ceftazidime 2 g / avibactam 0.5 g) every 8 hours

administration.label: Infusion duration
administration.value: Over 2 hours

administration.label: Renal adjustment
administration.value: Required for CrCl ≤50 mL/min

monitoring.labs: Monitor renal function — dose adjustment required for CrCl ≤50 mL/min; monitor CBC

clinicalAlerts: Infuse over 2 hours — not a short infusion
Dose adjustment required in renal impairment
Seizures and other CNS reactions reported with cephalosporins, especially in renal impairment
Not effective against MRSA

ceftobiprole.sourceNotes: Data extracted from FDA package insert for ZEVTERA (ceftobiprole medocaril sodium for injection) via DailyMed, with supplemental context from ASHP therapeutic update published in Am J Health Syst Pharm 2025.

reconstitution.label: Special Handling
reconstitution.value: White, yellowish to slightly brownish sterile powder; contains approximately 32 mg sodium per vial

dilution.label: Adult Concentration
dilution.value: 2.67 mg/mL

dilution.label: Pediatric Concentration (3 months to <12 years)
dilution.value: 5.33 mg/mL

dilution.label: Pediatric Concentration (12 to <18 years)
dilution.value: 2.67 mg/mL

administration.label: Route
administration.value: Intravenous infusion

administration.label: Infusion Duration
administration.value: 2 hours

administration.label: Rate Restrictions
administration.value: Administer over 2 hours at specified concentrations (2.67 mg/mL for adults and pediatric patients 12 to <18 years; 5.33 mg/mL for pediatric patients 3 months to <12 years)

clinicalAlerts: Not active against gram-negative bacteria producing extended-spectrum β-lactamases (ESBLs), serine carbapenemases (KPC), class B metallo-β-lactamases, high-level AmpC cephalosporinases, or Ambler class D β-lactamases including carbapenemases
Dosage adjustment required in adult patients with CrCl <50 mL/min and in pediatric patients aged 2 to <18 years with eGFR <50 mL/min/1.73 m²
Dosage increase required in adult patients with augmented renal clearance (CrCl >150 mL/min)
Ceftobiprole removed by hemodialysis; timing considerations may be necessary
Not approved for use in pediatric patients less than 3 months of age

citation.primary: ZEVTERA (ceftobiprole medocaril sodium for injection) [package insert]. FDA-approved labeling.
citation.ashp: Hitt EM, Bowers DR. Ceftobiprole: A therapeutic update. Am J Health Syst Pharm. 2025 May 23;82(11):e489-e497. doi: 10.1093/ajhp/zxaf006.

ceftolozane.drugClass: Cephalosporin antibacterial and beta-lactamase inhibitor combination
ceftolozane.sourceNotes: Data derived from FDA package insert (DailyMed setid 70ac1d90-eff3-4f0b-9f46-5846c571b32f) and supplemented with ASHP guidelines (Am J Health Syst Pharm. 2015 Dec 15;72(24):2135-46).

reconstitution.label: Vial size
reconstitution.value: 1.5 g (ceftolozane 1 g and tazobactam 0.5 g) per vial

reconstitution.label: Excipients
reconstitution.value: Sodium chloride (487 mg/vial), citric acid (21 mg/vial), L-arginine (approximately 600 mg/vial)

dilution.label: Administration
dilution.value: Intravenous infusion

administration.label: Route
administration.value: Intravenous infusion

administration.label: Infusion duration
administration.value: 1-hour intravenous infusion

administration.label: Dosing frequency
administration.value: Every 8 hours

administration.label: Standard dose for cIAI and cUTI
administration.value: 1.5 g (ceftolozane 1 g and tazobactam 0.5 g)

administration.label: Dose for HABP/VABP
administration.value: 3 g (ceftolozane 2 g and tazobactam 1 g)

clinicalAlerts: Dose adjustment required for renal impairment: CrCl 50-30 mL/min, 29-15 mL/min, and ESRD on hemodialysis
Approximately two-thirds of administered dose removed by hemodialysis; administer dose at earliest possible time following completion of HD on HD days
No dose adjustment needed for hepatic impairment or augmented renal function (CrCl ≥180 mL/min in HABP/VABP patients)
Elimination via renal excretion; ceftolozane eliminated by glomerular filtration, tazobactam via OAT1/OAT3 transporters
Protein binding: ceftolozane 16-21%, tazobactam 30%

citation.primary: Zerbaxa (ceftolozane and tazobactam) [package insert]. FDA DailyMed.
citation.ashp: Cluck D, Lewis P, Stayer B, Spivey J, Moorman J. Ceftolozane-tazobactam: A new-generation cephalosporin. Am J Health Syst Pharm. 2015 Dec 15;72(24):2135-46.

ceftriaxone.sourceNotes: Data extracted from FDA package insert (DailyMed setid 03283208-8616-4117-9d2b-aa8046017a4f) supplemented with ASHP guidelines for IV push administration from Brady et al. 2024.

reconstitution.label: Instructions
reconstitution.value: Reconstitute ceftriaxone sodium powder with appropriate diluent. Inject diluent into vial, shake vial thoroughly to form solution. Withdraw entire contents of vial into syringe to equal total labeled dose.

reconstitution.label: Resulting Concentration
reconstitution.value: After reconstitution, each 1 mL of solution contains approximately 250 mg or 350 mg

reconstitution.label: Solution pH
reconstitution.value: pH of 1% aqueous solution is approximately 6.7

reconstitution.label: Solution Appearance
reconstitution.value: Color ranges from light yellow to amber, depending on length of storage, concentration and diluent used

dilution.label: Compatible Diluents
dilution.value: Compatible fluid (calcium-free diluents only)

dilution.label: Contraindicated Diluents
dilution.value: Do NOT use diluents containing calcium, such as Ringer's solution or Hartmann's solution, to reconstitute ceftriaxone vials or to further dilute a reconstituted vial for IV administration because a precipitate can form

dilution.label: Stability Note
dilution.value: Precipitation of ceftriaxone-calcium can occur when ceftriaxone is mixed with calcium-containing solutions

administration.label: Routes
administration.value: Intravenous or intramuscular

administration.label: IV Infusion Duration - Adults
administration.value: 30 minutes (as documented in clinical pharmacology studies)

administration.label: IV Infusion Duration - Neonates
administration.value: 60 minutes minimum to reduce risk of bilirubin encephalopathy

administration.label: Calcium Interaction - General
administration.value: Must not be administered simultaneously with calcium-containing IV solutions, including continuous calcium-containing infusions such as parenteral nutrition via a Y-site

administration.label: Sequential Administration
administration.value: In patients other than neonates, ceftriaxone and calcium-containing solutions may be administered sequentially if infusion lines are thoroughly flushed between infusions with a compatible fluid

administration.label: Y-Site Administration
administration.value: Precipitation of ceftriaxone-calcium can occur when ceftriaxone is mixed with calcium-containing solutions in the same IV administration line

ivCompatibility.label: Calcium-containing solutions (Ringer's, Hartmann's)
ivCompatibility.value: Do not use - precipitate can form

ivCompatibility.label: Parenteral nutrition with calcium
ivCompatibility.value: Must not be administered simultaneously via Y-site

ivPush.maxConcentration: 250 mg/mL or 350 mg/mL (as documented for IM administration, applicable to IV push preparation)
ivPush.dilutionRequired: Reconstitute powder with appropriate calcium-free diluent
ivPush.specialConditions: ASHP guidelines document successful emergency department implementation of IV push ceftriaxone as alternative to IV piggyback, with significant time savings (order to administration decreased from 74 to 31 minutes). Must avoid all calcium-containing solutions.

monitoring.labs: Not specified in package insert
monitoring.drugLevels: Not required - therapeutic drug monitoring not indicated
monitoring.vitals: Not specified in package insert
monitoring.duringInfusion: Monitor for hypersensitivity reactions during infusion

sideEffects: Precipitation of ceftriaxone-calcium (when mixed with calcium-containing solutions)
Risk of bilirubin encephalopathy in neonates (especially with rapid administration)
clinicalAlerts: Contraindicated in premature neonates
Contraindicated in neonates ≤28 days requiring calcium-containing IV solutions
Hyperbilirubinemic neonates, especially prematures, should not be treated with ceftriaxone
Do not use calcium-containing diluents - precipitation can occur
Must not be administered simultaneously with calcium-containing IV solutions via Y-site
Infuse over 60 minutes in neonates to reduce risk of bilirubin encephalopathy
No dosage adjustment necessary for renal or hepatic impairment up to 2g/day

citation.primary: Ceftriaxone for Injection, USP Package Insert. DailyMed.
citation.ashp: Brady RE, Giordullo EL, Harvey CA, Krabacher ND, Penick AM. Intravenous push antibiotics in the emergency department: Education and implementation. Am J Health Syst Pharm. 2024 Jun 11;81(12):531-538.

cefuroxime.sourceNotes: Data extracted from FDA package insert (DailyMed setid=07170ee3-1a24-424f-a2a2-00bc92e98f85) for cefuroxime for injection and dextrose injection in DUPLEX container.

reconstitution.label: DUPLEX Container System
reconstitution.value: Product supplied as dual chamber container with crystalline cefuroxime sodium in drug chamber and dextrose injection in diluent chamber. Reconstitution achieved by activating seal between chambers. After removing foil strip, unfold container, fold below diluent meniscus, squeeze to open seal and release diluent into drug powder chamber. Agitate until drug powder completely dissolved.

reconstitution.label: Stability After Foil Strip Removal
reconstitution.value: Must be used within 7 days if foil strip removed, but not beyond labeled expiration date

reconstitution.label: Stability After Reconstitution
reconstitution.value: Must be used within 24 hours if stored at room temperature or within 7 days if refrigerated

dilution.label: Pre-mixed Product
dilution.value: Cefuroxime for Injection and Dextrose Injection in DUPLEX Container is a pre-mixed system designed to deliver 750 mg or 1.5 g dose after reconstitution. No additional dilution required.

administration.label: Route
administration.value: Intravenous only

administration.label: Method
administration.value: Intermittent IV infusion. Can be administered through Y-type administration set if patient receiving other IV solutions. During infusion of cefuroxime, temporarily discontinue administration of other solutions at same site.

administration.label: Special Precaution
administration.value: Do not use plastic containers in series connections due to risk of air embolism

administration.label: Temperature Before Use
administration.value: Do not use directly after refrigerated storage; allow product to equilibrate to room temperature before patient use

ivCompatibility.label: Aminoglycosides
ivCompatibility.value: Should not be added to solutions of aminoglycoside antibacterials because of potential interaction. Can be administered separately to same patient if concurrent therapy indicated.

ivCompatibility.label: Chloramphenicol
ivCompatibility.value: In vitro study showed antagonistic effects with combination of chloramphenicol and cefuroxime

monitoring.labs: Frequent bacteriologic and clinical appraisal necessary during therapy of chronic urinary tract infection and may be required for several months after therapy completed

clinicalAlerts: A reduced dosage must be employed when renal function is impaired
Patients on hemodialysis should be given a further dose at the end of dialysis
In staphylococcal and other infections involving a collection of pus, surgical drainage should be carried out where indicated
Pediatric dosing: To prevent unintentional overdose, this product should not be used in pediatric patients who require less than the full adult dose

citation.primary: Cefuroxime for Injection USP and Dextrose Injection USP FDA Package Insert

cenobamate.sourceNotes: Data extracted from FDA package insert (DailyMed setid=565c2126-57ae-4e29-b443-723bbe7e2072). XCOPRI is an oral-only formulation with no IV preparation available.

monitoring.labs: Obtain serum transaminases (ALT and AST) and total bilirubin prior to initiating therapy if not recently available (within 3 months) to establish baseline liver function

clinicalAlerts: XCOPRI is an oral tablet formulation only - no intravenous formulation exists
Drug Reaction with Eosinophilia and Systemic Symptoms (DRESS)/Multiorgan Hypersensitivity
QT Shortening - contraindicated in Familial Short QT syndrome
Suicidal Behavior and Ideation
Liver Injury - baseline hepatic function required
Maximum dosage 200 mg once daily in mild to moderate hepatic impairment; not recommended in severe hepatic impairment

citation.primary: XCOPRI (cenobamate) Package Insert. SK Life Science, Inc.

cerliponase alfa.sourceNotes: Data extracted from FDA package insert (DailyMed) for cerliponase alfa; no ASHP sources were available or needed for this specialized intraventricular enzyme replacement therapy.

administration.label: Route
administration.value: Intraventricular infusion via surgically implanted reservoir and catheter system (Codman HOLTER RICKHAM Reservoirs with Codman Ventricular Catheter)

administration.label: Dosing regimen
administration.value: Once every other week

administration.label: Infusion rate (birth to <6 months)
administration.value: 1.25 mL/hr for 100 mg dose (3.3 mL volume)

administration.label: Infusion rate (6 months to <1 year)
administration.value: 2.5 mL/hr for 150 mg dose (5 mL volume)

administration.label: Infusion rate (1 year to <2 years)
administration.value: 2.5 mL/hr for 200 mg (first 4 doses, 6.7 mL volume) then 300 mg (10 mL volume)

administration.label: Infusion rate (2 years and older)
administration.value: 2.5 mL/hr for 300 mg dose (10 mL volume)

administration.label: Total infusion duration
administration.value: Approximately 2 to 4.5 hours depending on dose and volume, including required Intraventricular Electrolytes infusion

administration.label: Sequence
administration.value: Administer BRINEURA first followed by infusion of Intraventricular Electrolytes

administration.label: Premedication
administration.value: Antihistamines with or without antipyretics or corticosteroids recommended 30 to 60 minutes prior to start of infusion

administration.label: Administration setting
administration.value: Healthcare setting with medical monitoring, support measures, and access to cardiopulmonary resuscitation equipment

administration.label: Supervision requirement
administration.value: Administered by or under supervision of physician experienced in intraventricular administration; supervised by healthcare provider knowledgeable in management of hypersensitivity reactions including anaphylaxis

administration.label: Pre-infusion inspection
administration.value: Inspect scalp for signs of intraventricular access device reservoir leakage, failure, or potential infection prior to each infusion

administration.label: CSF sampling
administration.value: Obtain CSF sample for cell count and culture prior to each infusion and when clinically indicated

administration.label: Aseptic technique
administration.value: Must be strictly observed during preparation and administration

administration.label: Device replacement
administration.value: Replace intraventricular access device reservoir prior to 4 years of single-puncture administrations

administration.label: Dosing restriction
administration.value: Not recommended in patients less than 37 weeks post-menstrual age or those weighing less than 2.5 kg

monitoring.labs: CSF cell count and culture prior to each infusion and when clinically indicated
monitoring.duringInfusion: Medical monitoring with access to cardiopulmonary resuscitation equipment

clinicalAlerts: Administration should be supervised by healthcare provider knowledgeable in management of hypersensitivity reactions including anaphylaxis
Initiate in healthcare setting with appropriate medical monitoring and support measures including access to cardiopulmonary resuscitation equipment
In event of severe hypersensitivity reaction (e.g., anaphylaxis), immediately discontinue administration and initiate appropriate medical treatment
If readministering after anaphylaxis, initiate subsequent infusion at approximately one-half the infusion rate at which anaphylactic reaction occurred with appropriate premedication
79% of patients developed anti-drug antibodies in serum and 42% in CSF; patients younger than 3 years had higher ADA titers
Hypersensitivity occurred in higher percentage in patients <3 years of age; one patient experienced anaphylaxis
Inspect scalp for signs of intraventricular access device reservoir leakage, failure, or potential infection prior to each infusion
Replace intraventricular access device reservoir prior to 4 years of single-puncture administrations

citation.primary: BRINEURA (cerliponase alfa) injection [package insert]. BioMarin Pharmaceutical Inc.

certolizumab.drugClass: TNF blocker (recombinant, humanized antibody Fab' fragment conjugated to polyethylene glycol)
certolizumab.sourceNotes: Data extracted from FDA package insert via DailyMed for certolizumab pegol (CIMZIA) describing subcutaneous formulations only.

reconstitution.label: Special Handling
reconstitution.value: Inspect visually for particulate matter and discoloration prior to administration. Solution should be clear to opalescent, colorless to yellow, essentially free from particulates. Do not use if cloudy or if foreign particulate matter is present. Does not contain preservatives; discard unused portions.

administration.label: Route
administration.value: Subcutaneous injection only

administration.label: Injection Site Rotation
administration.value: Rotate injection sites; do not inject into areas where skin is tender, bruised, red, or hard

administration.label: Multiple Injection Sites
administration.value: When 400 mg dose is needed (two subcutaneous injections of 200 mg), give injections at separate sites in the thigh or abdomen

administration.label: Preservatives
administration.value: Does not contain preservatives; discard unused portions of drug remaining in syringe or vial

citation.primary: CIMZIA (certolizumab pegol) [package insert]. U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

cetuximab.drugClass: Epidermal growth factor receptor (EGFR) antagonist; recombinant human/mouse chimeric monoclonal antibody
cetuximab.sourceNotes: Data extracted from FDA package insert (DailyMed) for ERBITUX (cetuximab) injection; ASHP guidelines provided context on combination regimens but did not contribute extractable IV administration data.

reconstitution.label: Formulation
reconstitution.value: Supplied as ready-to-use solution; no reconstitution required

reconstitution.label: Appearance
reconstitution.value: Clear, colorless solution which may contain a small amount of visible, white, amorphous cetuximab particulates

dilution.label: Dilution Required
dilution.value: No dilution required; administer as supplied

administration.label: Route
administration.value: Intravenous infusion only

administration.label: Administration Method
administration.value: Via infusion pump or syringe pump

administration.label: Do NOT Administer As
administration.value: IV push or bolus

administration.label: Infusion Rate Restriction
administration.value: Do not exceed 10 mg/min

administration.label: In-Line Filter
administration.value: Use low protein binding 0.22-micrometer in-line filter

administration.label: Initial Dose Infusion Duration
administration.value: 120 minutes (400 mg/m² weekly dosing or 500 mg/m² biweekly dosing)

administration.label: Subsequent Dose Infusion Duration
administration.value: 60 minutes (250 mg/m² weekly dosing)

administration.label: Timing with Radiation Therapy
administration.value: Complete ERBITUX administration 1 hour prior to radiation therapy

administration.label: Timing with Chemotherapy
administration.value: Complete ERBITUX administration 1 hour prior to chemotherapy

monitoring.labs: Serum electrolytes including serum magnesium, potassium, and calcium during and after ERBITUX administration
monitoring.duringInfusion: Monitor for infusion reactions during administration

sideEffects: Cutaneous adverse reactions (including rash, pruritus, and nail changes)
blackBoxWarnings: Infusion Reactions: ERBITUX can cause serious and fatal infusion reactions. Immediately interrupt and permanently discontinue ERBITUX for serious infusion reactions.

Cardiopulmonary Arrest: Cardiopulmonary arrest or sudden death occurred in patients with squamous cell carcinoma of the head and neck receiving ERBITUX with radiation therapy or a cetuximab product with platinum-based therapy and fluorouracil. Monitor serum electrolytes, including serum magnesium, potassium, and calcium, during and after ERBITUX administration.
clinicalAlerts: Premedicate with histamine-1 (H1) receptor antagonist IV 30–60 minutes prior to first dose or subsequent doses as deemed necessary
Immediately and permanently discontinue for Grade 3 or 4 infusion reactions
For Grade 1 or 2 infusion reactions, reduce infusion rate by 50%
Visually inspect for foreign particulate matter and discoloration prior to administration; do not use if solution is discolored, cloudy, or contains foreign particulate matter

citation.primary: ERBITUX (cetuximab) injection package insert. Eli Lilly and Company.
citation.ashp: Cersosimo RJ. Management of advanced colorectal cancer, Part 1 and Part 2. Am J Health Syst Pharm. 2013;70(5):395-406 and 70(6):491-506.

chenodiol.sourceNotes: Data extracted from FDA package insert accessed via DailyMed for chenodiol (CTEXLI) oral tablets; no IV formulation exists.

monitoring.labs: Baseline liver transaminase (ALT and AST) and total bilirubin levels required before initiating treatment; monitor liver transaminase and total bilirubin levels yearly and as clinically indicated

sideEffects: Abdominal pain including abdominal pain upper (14%)
Hypersensitivity reactions such as facial swelling, pruritus, rash, urticaria
clinicalAlerts: Hepatotoxicity: If liver transaminase (ALT, AST) levels are elevated > 3 times ULN or total bilirubin level is > 2 times ULN, interrupt treatment until levels return to baseline
Concomitant use with coumarin and its derivatives may cause unexpected prolongation of prothrombin time and hemorrhage; monitor prothrombin time if concomitant use is unavoidable
Avoid concomitant use with bile acid sequestering agents or aluminum-based antacids as they may decrease absorption and efficacy

chloramphenicol.sourceNotes: Data extracted from FDA package insert (DailyMed setid aed29594-211d-49ef-813f-131975a8d0e3); no ASHP supplementation required.

monitoring.labs: Adequate blood studies during treatment; monitor for peripheral blood changes including leukopenia, reticulocytopenia, or granulocytopenia

blackBoxWarnings: Serious and fatal blood dyscrasias (aplastic anemia, hypoplastic anemia, thrombocytopenia and granulocytopenia) are known to occur after the administration of chloramphenicol
Reports of aplastic anemia attributed to chloramphenicol which later terminated in leukemia
Blood dyscrasias have occurred after both short-term and prolonged therapy
Chloramphenicol must not be used when less potentially dangerous agents will be effective
Must not be used in treatment of trivial infections or where not indicated, as in colds, influenza, infections of the throat; or as prophylactic agent
Adequate blood studies essential during treatment; blood studies cannot be relied on to detect bone marrow depression prior to development of aplastic anemia
Desirable that patients be hospitalized to facilitate appropriate studies and observation during therapy
clinicalAlerts: Reserved for serious infections caused by susceptible organisms when less potentially hazardous therapeutic agents are ineffective or contraindicated
Each gram contains approximately 52 mg (2.25 mEq) of sodium
Irreversible bone marrow depression leading to aplastic anemia may appear weeks or months after therapy
Reversible dose-related bone marrow depression characterized by vacuolization of erythroid cells, reduction of reticulocytes and leukopenia responds promptly to withdrawal
Risk of fatal aplastic anemia estimated at 1:24,200 to 1:40,500
If optic or peripheral neuritis occurs, drug should be promptly withdrawn
Gray syndrome with toxic reactions including fatalities have occurred in premature and neonate

cidofovir.drugClass: Antiviral agent; CMV DNA polymerase inhibitor
cidofovir.sourceNotes: Data extracted from FDA package insert (DailyMed) for cidofovir injection; ASHP source referenced monkeypox use but provided no IV administration guidance applicable to this reference.

dilution.label: Diluent
dilution.value: 0.9% (normal) saline only

dilution.label: Volume
dilution.value: 100 mL

dilution.label: Final Concentration
dilution.value: Variable based on dose (appropriate volume of 75 mg/mL cidofovir added to 100 mL saline)

dilution.label: Stability
dilution.value: Administer within 24 hours of preparation; may refrigerate (2-8°C) for no more than 24 hours; equilibrate to room temperature before use

dilution.label: Special Handling
dilution.value: Mutagenic — prepare in Class II laminar flow biological safety cabinet; personnel must wear surgical gloves and closed front surgical gown with knit cuffs

administration.label: Route
administration.value: Intravenous infusion only — MUST NOT be administered by intraocular injection

administration.label: Infusion Duration
administration.value: 1 hour at a constant rate

administration.label: Equipment
administration.value: Standard infusion pump recommended

administration.label: Rate Restriction
administration.value: THE RECOMMENDED DOSAGE, FREQUENCY, OR INFUSION RATE MUST NOT BE EXCEEDED

administration.label: Required Concurrent Therapy — Probenecid
administration.value: 2 grams orally 3 hours before cidofovir; 1 gram at 2 hours after infusion; 1 gram at 8 hours after infusion (total 4 grams)

administration.label: Required Concurrent Therapy — Hydration
administration.value: At least 1 liter 0.9% saline IV over 1-2 hours immediately before cidofovir; second liter (if tolerated) at start or immediately after cidofovir, infused over 1-3 hours

ivCompatibility.label: 0.9% Saline
ivCompatibility.value: Compatible for dilution in polyvinyl chloride and ethylene/propylene copolymer infusion bags, and glass bottles

ivCompatibility.label: Ringer's Solution
ivCompatibility.value: Not evaluated

ivCompatibility.label: Lactated Ringer's
ivCompatibility.value: Not evaluated

ivCompatibility.label: Bacteriostatic Infusion Fluids
ivCompatibility.value: Not evaluated

ivCompatibility.label: Other Drugs/Supplements
ivCompatibility.value: No data available to support addition of other drugs or supplements for concurrent administration

monitoring.labs: Serum creatinine and urine protein within 48 hours prior to each dose; white blood cell counts with differential prior to each dose

clinicalAlerts: CONTRAINDICATED in patients with serum creatinine >1.5 mg/dL, calculated creatinine clearance ≤55 mL/min, or urine protein ≥100 mg/dL (≥2+ proteinuria)
Reduce maintenance dose from 5 mg/kg to 3 mg/kg for serum creatinine increase of 0.3-0.4 mg/dL above baseline
DISCONTINUE therapy for serum creatinine increase ≥0.5 mg/dL above baseline or development of ≥3+ proteinuria
Mutagenic — adequate precautions required for preparation, administration, and disposal
Probenecid and IV saline prehydration MUST be administered with each infusion to minimize nephrotoxicity

ciltacabtagene autoleucel.drugClass: BCMA-directed genetically modified autologous T cell immunotherapy
ciltacabtagene autoleucel.sourceNotes: Data extracted from FDA package insert (DailyMed setid 7d040b91-3fb8-41db-ba7f-60a36f06e2c2) dated per label sections 2, 11, and 12.

administration.label: Route
administration.value: Intravenous infusion only; autologous use only

administration.label: Preparation
administration.value: Thaw at 37°C ± 2°C using water bath or dry thaw method until no visible ice remains (no more than 15 minutes total thaw time)

administration.label: Infusion timing
administration.value: Once thawed, administer entire contents within 2.5 hours at room temperature (20°C to 25°C)

administration.label: Infusion technique
administration.value: Administer entire contents of bag by intravenous infusion using infusion sets fitted with an in-line filter. Gently mix contents of bag during infusion to disperse cell clumps

administration.label: Priming and flushing
administration.value: Prime tubing with normal saline prior to infusion. After entire bag is infused, flush administration line (including in-line filter) with normal saline volume equal to or greater than total hold-up volume of primary administration set

administration.label: Filter restriction
administration.value: Do NOT use a leukocyte-depleting filter

administration.label: Special handling
administration.value: Do not pre-filter into a different container, wash, spin down, or resuspend prior to infusion. Do not re-freeze or refrigerate thawed product

administration.label: Venous access
administration.value: Central venous access may be utilized and is encouraged in patients with poor peripheral access

administration.label: Premedication
administration.value: Administer 30–60 minutes prior to infusion: acetaminophen 650–1000 mg (oral or IV) and diphenhydramine 25–50 mg (oral or IV) or equivalent. Avoid prophylactic systemic corticosteroids

administration.label: Lymphodepleting regimen
administration.value: Cyclophosphamide 300 mg/m² IV and fludarabine 30 mg/m² IV daily for 3 days. Administer CARVYKTI infusion 2–4 days after completion of lymphodepleting regimen

administration.label: Pre-infusion requirements
administration.value: Confirm availability of tocilizumab and emergency equipment prior to infusion and during recovery period. Verify patient identity matches patient identifiers on cassette and infusion bag

ivCompatibility.label: Normal saline
ivCompatibility.value: Used to prime tubing and flush administration line

monitoring.vitals: Monitor for fever, hypoxia, and hypotension as signs of cytokine release syndrome
monitoring.duringInfusion: Monitor patients at least daily for 7 days following infusion for signs and symptoms of cytokine release syndrome (CRS) and neurologic toxicities

sideEffects: Elevated levels of IL-6, IL-10, IFN-γ, and IL-2 receptor alpha post-infusion (peak at Days 7–14)
clinicalAlerts: Instruct patients to remain within proximity of a healthcare facility for at least 2 weeks following infusion
Advise patients to avoid driving for at least 2 weeks following infusion
Delay lymphodepleting regimen if patient has serious adverse reactions from bridging therapies, active infection, cardiac/pulmonary toxicity, or active graft versus host disease

Delay CARVYKTI infusion if patient has clinically significant active infection/inflammatory disorders, Grade ≥3 non-hematologic toxicities from conditioning (except Grade 3 nausea/vomiting/diarrhea/constipation), until resolution to Grade ≤1
Consider repeating lymphodepleting regimen if CARVYKTI dosing delayed >14 days and patient recovered from toxicity of first lymphodepleting regimen
Follow universal precautions and local biosafety guidelines for handling and disposal

citation.primary: CARVYKTI (ciltacabtagene autoleucel) [package insert]. Horsham, PA: Janssen Biotech, Inc.

cinacalcet.sourceNotes: Data extracted exclusively from FDA package insert (DailyMed setid=02b8c11b-99e8-4039-b6fa-a9c38d2f48fa); ASHP sources address only extemporaneous oral suspension preparation and are not applicable to IV administration.

monitoring.labs: Serum calcium and serum phosphorus should be measured within 1 week after initiation or dose adjustment. Intact parathyroid hormone (iPTH) should be measured 1 to 4 weeks after initiation or dose adjustment, assessed no earlier than 12 hours after dosing. Once maintenance dose established, serum calcium should be measured approximately monthly for secondary HPT patients with CKD on dialysis, and every 2 months for parathyroid carcinoma or primary HPT patients.

clinicalAlerts: Contraindicated if serum calcium is less than the lower limit of normal range at treatment initiation
Hypocalcemia can occur; if serum calcium falls below 7.5 mg/dL or symptoms persist and vitamin D cannot be increased, withhold until calcium reaches 8.0 mg/dL and symptoms resolve
Upper gastrointestinal bleeding risk
Hypotension, worsening heart failure and/or arrhythmias may occur
Adynamic bone disease risk

ciprofloxacin.sourceNotes: Data extracted from FDA package insert (DailyMed) for Ciprofloxacin Injection, USP in 5% Dextrose. ASHP guidelines provided limited additional context on antimicrobial lock therapy but did not supplement primary data fields.

dilution.label: Ready-to-use solution
dilution.value: Supplied as sterile 0.2% ready-for-use infusion solution in 5% Dextrose Injection

dilution.label: Glucose content
dilution.value: 5 g per 100 mL bag; 10 g per 200 mL flexible container

administration.label: Route
administration.value: Intravenous infusion

administration.label: Infusion duration
administration.value: 60 minutes

administration.label: Adult dosing frequency
administration.value: Every 8 to 12 hours depending on indication

administration.label: Dose range
administration.value: 200 mg to 400 mg per dose

clinicalAlerts: Concomitant administration with tizanidine is contraindicated due to potentiation of hypotensive and sedative effects (Cmax 7-fold, AUC 10-fold increase)
Ciprofloxacin is an inhibitor of CYP1A2; co-administration with drugs metabolized by CYP1A2 may result in increased plasma concentrations and clinically significant adverse events
Monitoring for ropinirole-related adverse reactions recommended during co-administration (Cmax increased 60%, AUC increased 84%)
Careful monitoring of clozapine-associated adverse reactions advised during co-administration (serum concentrations increased by 29%)
Use sildenafil with caution when co-administered; expect two-fold increase in sildenafil exposure

monitoring.drugLevels: Laudanosine metabolite accumulation may occur with prolonged administration in patients with renal or hepatic impairment; mean t½β of laudanosine is 3.1 ± 0.4 hours
monitoring.vitals: Monitor mean arterial blood pressure (MAP) and heart rate (HR) during administration
monitoring.duringInfusion: Monitor neuromuscular function with peripheral nerve stimulator to assess degree of blockade and recovery; monitor train-of-four ratio

clinicalAlerts: Should be administered only by or under supervision of experienced clinicians familiar with use of neuromuscular blocking agents
Patients with myasthenia gravis or myasthenic syndrome may have profound and prolonged neuromuscular blockade
Metabolites (laudanosine) may accumulate with prolonged administration in patients with renal or hepatic impairment
Prolonged neuromuscular blockade may occur in ICU patients receiving long-term infusions
